title.raw: 卷五 孝安帝紀 Volume 5: Annals of Emperor Xiao'an

sentences.zh: 恭宗孝安皇帝諱祜，肅宗孫也。

translations.literal: Emperor Gongzong, the Filial and Secure Emperor, whose taboo name was Hu, was a grandson of Emperor Suzong (Zhang).
translations.idiomatic: Emperor An, posthumously honored as Gongzong, whose personal name was Hu, was a grandson of Emperor Zhang.

sentences.zh: 父清河孝王慶，母左姬。

translations.literal: His father was the Filial King Qing of Qinghe; his mother was Concubine Zuo.
translations.idiomatic: His father was Prince Qing of Qinghe; his mother was Lady Zuo.

sentences.zh: 帝自在邸第，數有神光照室，又有赤蛇盤於床笫之間。

translations.literal: While the emperor was still in the princely residence, repeatedly there was divine light illuminating the chamber, and moreover a red snake coiled between the bed boards.
translations.idiomatic: While he still lived in the princely mansion, supernatural light was often seen in his rooms and a red snake coiled among the bedsteads.

sentences.zh: 年十歲，好學《史書》，和帝稱之，數見禁中。

translations.literal: At age ten he loved studying the 'Scribe Documents'; Emperor He praised him and repeatedly saw him within the forbidden precincts.
translations.idiomatic: At ten he loved studying clerical script; Emperor He praised him and often summoned him to the palace.

sentences.zh: 延平元年，慶始就國，鄧太后特詔留帝清河邸。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yanping, Qing for the first time went to his state; Empress Dowager Deng by special edict kept the emperor at the Qinghe princely residence.
translations.idiomatic: In 106 CE Prince Qing left for his fief, but Empress Dowager Deng issued a special order keeping the boy at the Qinghe princely mansion in the capital.

sentences.zh: 八月，殤帝崩，太后與兄車騎將軍鄧騭定策禁中。

translations.literal: Eighth month, Emperor Shang collapsed; the empress dowager with her elder brother General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Zhi fixed the policy within the forbidden precincts.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Emperor Shang died; inside the palace the empress dowager and her brother Deng Zhi, general of chariots and cavalry, settled the succession.

sentences.zh: 其夜，使騭持節，以王青蓋車迎帝，齋於殿中。

translations.literal: That night, she sent Zhi bearing the staff of authority, with the king's green-canopy carriage to welcome the emperor; he fasted in the hall.
translations.idiomatic: That night Deng Zhi, bearing credentials, brought the imperial green-canopy carriage for the boy, who fasted in the hall before accession.

sentences.zh: 皇太后御崇德殿，百官皆吉服，群臣陪位，引拜帝為長安侯。

translations.literal: The empress dowager presided at Chongde Hall; the hundred officials all wore auspicious dress; the host of ministers attended in their places; he was led in and invested as marquis of Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager took her seat in Chongde Hall in auspicious dress; the ministers stood in attendance as the boy was led forward and named Marquis of Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 皇太后詔曰：『先帝聖德淑茂，早弃天下。

translations.literal: The empress dowager issued an edict saying: 'The late emperor's sage virtue was pure and lush; he early abandoned all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager proclaimed: the late sovereign was full of sage virtue, yet Heaven took him while he was still young.

sentences.zh: 朕奉皇帝，夙夜瞻仰日月，冀望成就。

translations.literal: We received and served the emperor, day and night gazing up at sun and moon, hoping for completion.
translations.idiomatic: I held him on the throne and watched over him night and day, hoping he would grow into the task.

sentences.zh: 豈意卒然顛沛，天年不遂，悲痛斷心。

translations.literal: Who could have thought he would suddenly be overturned and cast down, his heavenly years not fulfilled—grief and pain break Our heart.
translations.idiomatic: Who could have foreseen such a sudden fall—his years unfulfilled? My heart breaks with grief.

sentences.zh: 朕惟平原王素被痼疾，念宗廟之重，思繼嗣之統，唯長安侯祜質性忠孝，小心翼翼，能通《詩》、《論》，篤學樂古，仁惠愛下。

translations.literal: We consider that the king of Pingyuan has long suffered chronic illness; pondering the weight of the ancestral temples, thinking on the thread of heir and successor—only Marquis of Chang'an Hu in native substance is loyal and filial, cautious and careful as if holding something fragile; he can penetrate the 'Odes' and 'Analects'; he loves study and delights in antiquity; he is benevolent, kind, and cherishes those below.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Sheng of Pingyuan has long been gravely ill; the altars need an heir, and only Marquis Hu of Chang'an combines loyalty and filial piety with careful learning of the Odes and Analects, love of antiquity, and kindness to others.

sentences.zh: 年已十三，有成人之志。

translations.literal: His years are already thirteen; he has the resolve of an adult.
translations.idiomatic: He is thirteen and already shows a grown man's steadiness.

sentences.zh: 親德係後，莫宜於祜。

translations.literal: In closeness of virtue tying the successor, none is more fitting than Hu.
translations.idiomatic: For virtue and fitness to continue the line, none matches Hu.

sentences.zh: 《禮》「昆弟之子猶己子」；

translations.literal: The 'Rites' says, 'The sons of elder and younger brothers are like one's own sons';
translations.idiomatic: The Rites teach that a brother's son may stand as one's own child;

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之義，為人後者為之子，不以父命辭王父命。

translations.literal: the meaning of the 'Spring and Autumn' is that one who becomes another's heir becomes his son, and does not decline the grandfather's command on the father's command.
translations.idiomatic: the Spring and Autumn tradition makes an adopted heir the son of his new line, who may not reject the grandfather's charge for the father's sake.

sentences.zh: 其以祜為孝和皇帝嗣，奉承祖宗，案禮儀奏。

translations.literal: Let Hu be made heir of the Filial and Harmonious Emperor, receiving and serving the ancestors; investigate ritual and submit memorials.
translations.idiomatic: Let Hu succeed Emperor He, serve the ancestors, and let the rites be reported accordingly.

sentences.zh: 』又作策命曰：『惟延平元年秋八月癸丑，皇太后曰：咨長安侯祜：孝和皇帝懿德巍巍，光於四海；

translations.literal: ' Moreover a planked mandate was made, saying: 'It is the first year of Yanping, autumn, eighth month, guichou day; the empress dowager says: Ah, Marquis of Chang'an Hu: the Filial and Harmonious Emperor's fine virtue was lofty, his radiance reached within the four seas;
translations.idiomatic: A written mandate added: on 23 September 106 CE the empress dowager addressed Marquis Hu of Chang'an—Emperor He's noble virtue shone across the realm;

sentences.zh: 大行皇帝不永天年。

translations.literal: the late traveling emperor did not prolong his heavenly years.
translations.idiomatic: yet the late emperor did not live out his years.

sentences.zh: 朕惟侯孝章帝世嫡皇孫，謙恭慈順，在孺而勤，宜奉郊廟，承統大業。

translations.literal: We consider that the marquis is the Filial Zhang Emperor's generation's legitimate imperial grandson, modest, reverent, kind, and compliant; in childhood yet diligent—fit to serve suburb and temple altars, to receive the thread of the great enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: You are Emperor Zhang's legitimate great-grandson, humble and diligent even as a child—fit to sacrifice at the altars and bear the great mandate.

sentences.zh: 今以侯嗣孝和皇帝後。

translations.literal: Now We make the marquis heir after the Filial and Harmonious Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: We therefore name you heir to Emperor He.

sentences.zh: 其審君漢國，允執其中。

translations.literal: Examine and rule the Han state; sincerely hold its center.
translations.idiomatic: Govern the Han house with care and hold fast to the mean.

sentences.zh: 「一人有慶，萬民賴之。

translations.literal: "When one man has blessing, the myriad people rely on him.
translations.idiomatic: When the sovereign is blessed, the people have their stay;

sentences.zh: 」皇帝其勉之哉！

translations.literal: " Emperor, strive in this!
translations.idiomatic: therefore strive, Son of Heaven!"

sentences.zh: 』讀策畢，太尉奉上璽綬，即皇帝位，年十三。

translations.literal: ' When the reading of the mandate was finished, the Grand Commandant presented the seal and ribbon; he assumed the imperial throne; he was thirteen sui.
translations.idiomatic: When the text had been read, the Grand Commandant presented the imperial seal and cord; Hu ascended the throne at the age of thirteen.

sentences.zh: 太后猶臨朝。

translations.literal: The empress dowager still presided over court.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager continued to rule as regent.

sentences.zh: 九月庚子，謁高廟。

translations.literal: Ninth month, gengzi day, he visited Gao's temple.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 September 106 CE he paid homage at the temple of Emperor Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，謁光武廟。

translations.literal: On the xinchou day, he visited Guangwu's temple.
translations.idiomatic: On the following day he visited Emperor Guangwu's temple.

sentences.zh: 六州大水。

translations.literal: Six provinces had great flood.
translations.idiomatic: Six provinces were inundated.

sentences.zh: 己未，遣謁者分行虛實，舉灾害，賑乏絕。

translations.literal: On the jiwei day, messengers were sent separately to go examine truth and falsehood, raise disasters and harms, and relieve exhaustion and cutoff.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 October envoys toured the provinces to verify reports, record disasters, and relieve the destitute.

sentences.zh: 丙寅，葬孝殤皇帝於康陵。

translations.literal: On the bingyin day, the Filial and Early-Departed Emperor was buried at Kang Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 October Emperor Shang was buried at the Kang mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 乙亥，隕石於陳留。

translations.literal: On the yihai day, a meteorite fell in Chenliu.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 October a meteorite fell in Chenliu commandery.

sentences.zh: 西域諸國叛，攻都護任尚，遣副校尉梁慬救尚，擊破之。

translations.literal: The various states of the Western Regions rebelled and attacked Protector-General Ren Shang; Deputy Colonel Liang Jin was sent to rescue Shang and struck and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Western Regions states rebelled and besieged Ren Shang; Deputy Colonel Liang Jin relieved him and routed the rebels.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，四州大水，雨雹。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, four provinces had great flood and rained hail.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter four provinces flooded and hail fell.

sentences.zh: 詔以宿麥不下，賑賜貧人。

translations.literal: It was ordered that because winter wheat did not come down, poor people be granted relief.
translations.idiomatic: Because winter wheat had failed to sprout, an edict ordered relief for the poor.

sentences.zh: 十二月甲子，清河王薨，使司空持節吊祭，車騎將軍鄧騭護喪事。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, jiazi day, the king of Qinghe died; the Minister of Works was sent bearing the staff to condole and sacrifice; General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Zhi supervised the funeral affairs.
translations.idiomatic: On 17 December 106 CE Prince Qing of Qinghe died; the Minister of Works was sent to mourn, and Deng Zhi supervised the funeral.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，罷魚龍曼延百戲。

translations.literal: On the yiyou day, the fish-dragon spreading-stretch hundred entertainments were abolished.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 January 107 CE the court ended the elaborate fish-and-dragon variety shows.

sentences.zh: 永初元年春正月癸酉朔，大赦天下。

translations.literal: First year of Yongchu, spring, first month, guiyou new-moon day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: At the new year of 107 CE, Yongchu 1, a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 蜀郡徼外羌內屬。

translations.literal: Qiang beyond the passes of Shu commandery attached inward.
translations.idiomatic: Qiang beyond Shu commandery submitted to Han.

sentences.zh: 戊寅，分犍為南部為屬國都尉。

translations.literal: On the wuyin day, the southern part of Qianwei was divided off as dependent-state commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 February 107 CE the southern portion of Qianwei was organized as a dependent state under a commandant.

sentences.zh: 稟司隸、兗、豫、徐、冀、幷州貧民。

translations.literal: Grain was granted to poor commoners of the Metropolitan Province, Yan, Yu, Xu, Ji, and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: The court distributed grain to the poor in the capital region and in Yan, Yu, Xu, Ji, and Bing.

sentences.zh: 二月丙午，以廣成遊獵地及被灾郡國公田假與貧民。

translations.literal: Second month, bingwu day, the Guangcheng hunting preserve and public fields of disaster-struck commanderies and kingdoms were lent to poor commoners.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 March 107 CE the Guangcheng hunting park and government fields in disaster areas were lent to the poor.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，分清河國封帝弟常保為廣川王。

translations.literal: On the dingmao day, Qinghe state was divided to enfeoff the emperor's younger brother Changbao as king of Guangchuan.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 April territory was taken from Qinghe to make the emperor's brother Changbao prince of Guangchuan.

sentences.zh: 庚午，司徒梁鮪薨。

translations.literal: On the gengwu day, Minister of Education Liang Wei died.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 April 107 CE Minister of Education Liang Wei died.

sentences.zh: 三月癸酉，日有食之，詔公卿內外眾官、郡國守、相，舉賢良方正、有道術之士，明政術、達古今、能直言極諫者，各一人。

translations.literal: Third month, guiyou day, there was an eclipse of the sun; it was ordered that dukes and ministers, inner and outer host of officials, commandery and kingdom governors and chancellors each recommend one worthy and good, upright and correct, possessed of the way and arts, clear in government arts, penetrating ancient and modern, able to speak bluntly and remonstrate to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse on 9 April 107 CE prompted an order for every high office and commandery to nominate one man noted for integrity, learning, grasp of statecraft, and fearless counsel.

sentences.zh: 己卯，永昌徼外僬僥種夷貢獻內屬。

translations.literal: On the jimao day, beyond the passes of Yongchang the Jiaoyao-tribe Yi presented tribute and attached inward.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 April the Jiaoyao people beyond Yongchang sent tribute and submitted.

sentences.zh: 甲申，葬清河孝王，贈龍旗、虎賁。

translations.literal: On the jiashen day, the Filial King of Qinghe was buried; dragon banners and tiger runners were granted.
translations.idiomatic: On 20 April Prince Qing of Qinghe was buried with dragon banners and a guard of picked warriors.

sentences.zh: 夏五月甲戌，長樂衛尉魯恭為司徒。

translations.literal: Summer, fifth month, jiaxu day, Commandant of the Guards of Changle Lu Gong became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 May 107 CE Lu Gong, commandant of the Changle guards, became Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，詔封北海王睦孫壽光侯普為北海王。

translations.literal: On the dingchou day, it was ordered to enfeff the grandson of King Mu of Beihai, Marquis Pu of Shouguang, as king of Beihai.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 May Liu Pu, Marquis of Shouguang and grandson of Prince Mu of Beihai, was made prince of Beihai.

sentences.zh: 九真徼外夜郎蠻夷舉土內屬。

translations.literal: Beyond the passes of Jiuzhen, Yelang Man and Yi raised their territory and attached inward.
translations.idiomatic: Yelang tribes beyond Jiuzhen surrendered their lands to Han.

sentences.zh: 六月戊申，爵皇太后母陰氏為新野君。

translations.literal: Sixth month, wushen day, the empress dowager's mother nee Yin was ennobled as lord of Xinye.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 June 107 CE the empress dowager's mother, Lady Yin, received the title of Lady of Xinye.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，河東地陷。

translations.literal: On the dingsi day, Hedong's ground sank.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 June the earth subsided in Hedong.

sentences.zh: 壬戌，罷西域都護。

translations.literal: On the renxu day, the Protector-General of the Western Regions was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 July the office of Protector-General of the Western Regions was abolished.

sentences.zh: 先零種羌叛，斷隴道，大為寇掠，遣車騎將軍鄧騭、征西校尉任尚討之。

translations.literal: The Qiang of the Xianling tribe rebelled, cut the Long road, and greatly raided and plundered; General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Zhi and Colonel Who Conquers the West Ren Shang were sent to punish them.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianling Qiang rebelled, blocked the Long Mountain route, and raided widely; Deng Zhi and Ren Shang were sent against them.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，赦除諸羌相連結謀叛逆者罪。

translations.literal: On the dingmao day, guilt was remitted for the various Qiang who had linked and plotted rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 July those Qiang who had joined the plot were offered amnesty.

sentences.zh: 秋九月庚午，詔三公明申舊令，禁奢侈，無作浮巧之物，殫財厚葬。

translations.literal: Autumn, ninth month, gengwu day, it was ordered that the Three Dukes clearly reiterate old ordinances, prohibit extravagance, make no floating clever goods, and exhaust wealth on thick burials.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 September 107 CE the Three Dukes were told to reissue old sumptuary laws against luxury, frivolous crafts, and lavish funerals.

sentences.zh: 是日，太尉徐防免。

translations.literal: That same day, Grand Commandant Xu Fang was removed.
translations.idiomatic: That day Grand Commandant Xu Fang was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 辛未，司空尹勤免。

translations.literal: On the xinwei day, Minister of Works Yin Qin was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 September Minister of Works Yin Qin was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，調楊州五郡租米，贍給東郡、濟陰、陳留、梁國、下邳、山陽。

translations.literal: On the guiyou day, rent rice of five commanderies of Yang province was transferred to supply Dong, Jiyin, Chenliu, Liang state, Xiapi, and Shanyang.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 September grain tax from five Yang commanderies was shipped to feed Dong, Jiyin, Chenliu, Liang, Xiapi, and Shanyang.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，詔曰：『自今長吏被考竟未報，自非父母喪，無故輒去職者，劇縣十歲、平縣五歲以上，乃得次用。

translations.literal: On the dingchou day an edict said: 'From now on, when senior clerks are under investigation to the end and not yet reported, unless it is parents' mourning, those who without cause then leave office—in a severe commandery ten years, in a level commandery five years and above, then they may be used in order.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 September an edict ruled that magistrates under investigation who quit without cause—not for parents' funeral—must wait ten years in a difficult county or five in an ordinary one before reinstatement.

sentences.zh: 』壬午，詔太僕、少府減黃門鼓吹，以補羽林士；

translations.literal: ' On the renwu day, it was ordered that the Grand Coachman and Privy Treasurer reduce the Yellow Gate wind-and-percussion bands, to supplement Forest of Feathers knights;
translations.idiomatic: On 24 September the Grand Coachman and Privy Treasurer were ordered to cut the palace wind bands and transfer the men to the Forest of Feathers guard;

sentences.zh: 廄馬非乘輿常所御者，皆減半食；

translations.literal: stable horses not those the carriage-and-cap normally drives—all reduce feed by half;
translations.idiomatic: stable horses not used for the imperial carriage had their fodder halved;

sentences.zh: 諸所造作，非供宗廟園陵之用，皆且止。

translations.literal: all constructions made, not for supplying ancestral temples and park tumuli, all for the time being stop.
translations.idiomatic: all building projects not needed for shrines or imperial tombs were suspended.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，詔死罪以下及亡命贖，各有差。

translations.literal: On the bingxu day, it was ordered that from death crime downward and fugitives with warrant, ransom, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 September an edict allowed commutation by ransom for crimes short of capital and for fugitives, on a graded scale.

sentences.zh: 庚寅，太傅張禹為太尉，太常周章為司空。

translations.literal: On the gengyin day, Grand Tutor Zhang Yu became Grand Commandant; Grand Master of Ceremonies Zhou Zhang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 2 October Zhang Yu moved from Grand Tutor to Grand Commandant, and Zhou Zhang from Grand Master of Ceremonies to Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，倭國遣使奉獻。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, the Wa state sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter Wa sent envoys with tribute.

sentences.zh: 辛酉，新城山泉水大出。

translations.literal: On the xinyou day, at Xincheng mountain spring water gushed out greatly.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 November a great spring burst from the mountain at Xincheng.

sentences.zh: 十一月丁亥，司空周章密謀廢立，策免，自殺。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, dinghai day, Minister of Works Zhou Zhang secretly plotted deposition and installation; he was dismissed by rescript and committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 December 107 CE Zhou Zhang, Minister of Works, was caught plotting to depose the emperor; he was stripped of office and killed himself.

sentences.zh: 戊子，勑司隸校尉、冀、幷二州刺史：『民訛言相驚，弃捐舊居，老弱相攜，窮困道路。

translations.literal: On the wuzi day, the Metropolitan Superintendent and the two provincial governors of Ji and Bing were charged: 'The people by false rumors frighten one another, abandon their old dwellings, old and weak lead one another, destitute on the roads.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 December the Metropolitan Superintendent and the governors of Ji and Bing were told that panic rumors had driven people from their homes, young and old wandering destitute on the roads.

sentences.zh: 其各勑所部長吏，躬親曉喻。

translations.literal: Let each order his department's senior clerks personally to go and enlighten.
translations.idiomatic: Each was to order local magistrates to go in person and calm the people.

sentences.zh: 若欲歸本郡，在所為封長檄；

translations.literal: If they wish to return to their home commandery, where they are let them seal a long pass;
translations.idiomatic: Those who wished to return home were to be given sealed travel passes;

sentences.zh: 不欲，勿強。』

translations.literal: if they do not wish, do not force.'
translations.idiomatic: those who did not wish to go were not to be compelled.

sentences.zh: 十二月乙卯，潁川太守張敏為司空。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, yimao day, Governor of Yingchuan Zhang Min became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 January 108 CE Zhang Min, governor of Yingchuan, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 是歲，郡國十八地震；

translations.literal: That year, eighteen commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes;
translations.idiomatic: That year eighteen commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 四十一雨水，或山水暴至；

translations.literal: forty-one had rain and water, or mountain waters suddenly arrived;
translations.idiomatic: Forty-one commanderies saw flooding or sudden mountain torrents.

sentences.zh: 二十八大風，雨雹。

translations.literal: twenty-eight had great wind and rained hail.
translations.idiomatic: Twenty-eight commanderies were struck by gales and hail.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，稟河南、下邳、東萊、河內貧民。

translations.literal: Second year, spring, first month, grain was granted to poor commoners of Henan, Xiapi, Donglai, and Henei.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 108 CE, the court issued grain to the poor in Henan, Xiapi, Donglai, and Henei.

sentences.zh: 車騎將軍鄧騭為種羌所敗於冀西。

translations.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Zhi was by the Zhong Qiang defeated west of Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Zhi was defeated west of Ji by the Zhong Qiang.

sentences.zh: 二月乙丑，遣光祿大夫樊準、呂倉分行冀、兗二州，稟貸流民。

translations.literal: Second month, yichou day, Household Grandees Fan Zhun and Lü Cang were sent separately to tour the two provinces of Ji and Yan, granting grain loans to floating people.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 March 108 CE Fan Zhun and Lü Cang toured Ji and Yan to lend grain to vagrants.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲寅，漢陽城中火，燒殺三千五百七十人。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, jiayin day, in Hanyang city there was fire; burned and killed three thousand five hundred seventy people.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 May 108 CE a fire in Hanyang city killed 3,570 people.

sentences.zh: 五月，旱。

translations.literal: Fifth month, drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month drought struck.

sentences.zh: 丙寅，皇太后幸洛陽寺及若廬獄，錄囚徒，賜河南尹、廷尉、卿及官屬以下各有差，即日降雨。

translations.literal: On the bingyin day, the empress dowager favored Luoyang prison and Ruolu prison with her presence, recorded prisoners, bestowed on the Governor of Henan, Commandant of Justice, ministers, and official subordinates below each according to gradation; that same day rain fell.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 June 108 CE the empress dowager visited Luoyang jail and Ruolu prison, reviewed cases, and rewarded the governor of Henan, the commandant of justice, and their staffs; rain fell the same day.

sentences.zh: 六月，京師及郡國四十大水，大風，雨雹。

translations.literal: Sixth month, the capital region and forty commanderies and kingdoms had great flood, great wind, and rained hail.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the capital and forty commanderies suffered floods, gales, and hail.

sentences.zh: 秋七月戊辰，詔曰：『昔在帝王，承天理民，莫不據璇機玉衡，以齊七政。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, wuchen day, an edict said: 'Of old the emperors and kings, receiving Heaven and ordering the people, none did not rely on the jade sight-tube armillary and jade transverse, thereby to align the seven powers.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 August 108 CE an edict recalled that ancient kings aligned the seven celestial powers with the armillary sphere when governing the people.

sentences.zh: 朕以不德，遵奉大業，而陰陽差越，變異並見，萬民飢流，羌貊叛戾。

translations.literal: We, lacking virtue, have received and upheld the great enterprise, yet yin and yang miss and cross; prodigies appear together; the myriad people hunger and drift; Qiang and Mo rebel and are perverse.
translations.idiomatic: I lack virtue yet bear the great charge; yin and yang are out of joint, omens multiply, the people starve and flee, and Qiang and Mo rebel.

sentences.zh: 夙夜克己，憂心京京。

translations.literal: Day and night We restrain self; Our anxious heart is vast.
translations.idiomatic: Night and day I discipline myself; anxiety fills my breast.

sentences.zh: 間令公卿郡國舉賢良方正，遠求博選，開不諱之路，冀得至謀，以鑒不逮，而所對皆循尚浮言，無卓爾異聞。

translations.literal: Recently We ordered dukes and ministers and commanderies and kingdoms to raise worthy and good, upright and correct, seeking far and selecting broadly, opening a road of no taboo, hoping to obtain supreme plans to mirror Our insufficiency—yet those who responded all followed and esteemed empty words, with nothing outstanding or different heard.
translations.idiomatic: I ordered broad recruitment of worthy men and an open road for blunt counsel, yet the answers have been empty convention, nothing striking or new.

sentences.zh: 其百僚及郡國吏人，有道術明習灾異陰陽之度璇機之數者，各使指變以聞。

translations.literal: Let the hundred officials and commandery and kingdom clerks and commoners who have the way and arts and clearly practice the measures of disaster prodigies, yin and yang, and the reckonings of the armillary—each cause them to point to the changes and report.
translations.idiomatic: Let every official and subject who understands omens, yin and yang, and astronomical reckoning report what these changes portend.

sentences.zh: 二千石長吏明以詔書，博衍幽隱，朕將親覽，待以不次，冀獲嘉謀，以承天誡。』

translations.literal: Two-thousand-dan senior clerks, clearly with the edict text, broadly unfold the secluded and hidden; We will personally peruse, treat with non-sequential promotion, hoping to obtain fine plans, thereby to receive Heaven's warning.'
translations.idiomatic: Let governors publish this edict and bring forward hidden talent; I will read the memorials myself and promote out of turn for good counsel that answers Heaven's rebuke.

sentences.zh: 閏月辛丑，廣川王常保薨，無子，國除。

translations.literal: Intercalary month, xinchou day, King Changbao of Guangchuan died; having no son, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 September 108 CE Prince Changbao of Guangchuan died without an heir, and his fief was abolished.

sentences.zh: 癸未，蜀郡徼外羌舉土內屬。

translations.literal: On the guiwei day, Qiang beyond the passes of Shu commandery raised their territory and attached inward.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 October Qiang beyond Shu surrendered their territory to Han.

sentences.zh: 九月庚子，詔王國官屬墨綬下至郎、謁者，其經明任博士，居鄉里有廉清孝順之稱、才任理人者，國相歲移名，與計偕上尚書，公府通調，令得外補。

translations.literal: Ninth month, gengzi day, it was ordered that in princely states, official subordinates from black-ribbon rank down to Gentlemen and messengers—those whose classics are clear and who could serve as erudits, who in village and hamlet have a reputation for incorrupt purity and filial obedience, whose talent fits governing people—the kingdom chancellor each year shall transfer their names, going up with the accounting to the Masters of Writing; the public offices shall coordinate transfer, ordering that they may be supplemented outside.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 October 108 CE an edict allowed princely chancellors to nominate learned subordinates of erudit rank for posting outside the fief via the annual accounting and the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 冬十月庚寅，稟濟陰、山陽、玄菟貧民。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, gengyin day, grain was granted to poor commoners of Jiyin, Shanyang, and Xuantu.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 November 108 CE grain was issued to the poor in Jiyin, Shanyang, and Xuantu.

sentences.zh: 征西校尉任尚與先零羌戰于平襄，尚軍敗績。

translations.literal: Colonel Who Conquers the West Ren Shang fought the Xianling Qiang at Pingxiang; Shang's army was badly defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Shang fought the Xianling Qiang at Pingxiang and suffered a crushing defeat.

sentences.zh: 十一月辛酉，拜鄧騭為大將軍，徵還京師，留任尚屯隴右。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, xinyou day, Deng Zhi was appointed Grand General, summoned back to the capital; Ren Shang was left to encamp on the Long right.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 December 108 CE Deng Zhi was made Grand General and recalled to the capital while Ren Shang remained to garrison the Longxi region.

sentences.zh: 先零羌滇零稱天子於北地，遂寇三輔，東犯趙、魏，南入益州，殺漢中太守董炳。

translations.literal: The Xianling Qiang Dianling declared himself Son of Heaven in Beidi, then raided the Three Adjuncts, east violated Zhao and Wei, south entered Yizhou, and killed Governor of Hanzhong Dong Bing.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianling leader Dianling declared himself emperor in Beidi, raided the capital region, struck east into Zhao and Wei, pushed south into Yi province, and killed Dong Bing, governor of Hanzhong.

sentences.zh: 十二月辛卯，稟東郡、鉅鹿、廣陽、安定、定襄、沛國貧民。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, xinmao day, grain was granted to poor commoners of Dong, Julu, Guangyang, Anding, Dingxiang, and Pei state.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 January 109 CE grain was distributed to the poor in Dong, Julu, Guangyang, Anding, Dingxiang, and Pei.

sentences.zh: 廣漢塞外參狼羌降，分廣漢北部為屬國都尉。

translations.literal: Beyond the passes of Guanghan the Sanlang Qiang surrendered; the northern part of Guanghan was divided off as dependent-state commandant.
translations.idiomatic: The Sanlang Qiang beyond Guanghan submitted, and the northern part of Guanghan was made a dependent state under a commandant.

sentences.zh: 是歲，郡國十二地震。

translations.literal: That year, twelve commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: That year twelve commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月庚子，皇帝加元服。

translations.literal: Third year, spring, first month, gengzi day, the emperor added the cap-of-adulthood.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 February 109 CE the emperor came of age.

sentences.zh: 大赦天下。

translations.literal: A general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty was proclaimed throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 賜王、主、貴人、公、卿以下金帛各有差；

translations.literal: To kings, princesses, honored ladies, dukes, and ministers down, gold and silk were bestowed, each according to gradation;
translations.idiomatic: Princes, princesses, imperial ladies, dukes, and ministers received graded gifts of gold and silk;

sentences.zh: 男子為父後，及三老、考悌、力田爵，人二級，流民欲占者人一級。

translations.literal: males who were heirs to fathers, and the three elders, examplars of filial piety and fraternal duty, and strength-in-farming noble ranks, each two grades; floating people who wished to register, one grade each.
translations.idiomatic: male heirs, the three elders, models of filial piety and fraternal duty, and outstanding farmers each received two steps of honorary rank, and vagrants who registered for residence one step.

sentences.zh: 遣騎都尉任仁討先零羌，不利，羌遂破沒臨洮。

translations.literal: Colonel of Cavalry Ren Ren was sent to punish the Xianling Qiang; it went ill; the Qiang then smashed and drowned Lintao.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Ren Ren campaigned against the Xianling Qiang without success; the Qiang overran and sacked Lintao.

sentences.zh: 高句驪遣使貢獻。

translations.literal: Gaogouli sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Koguryŏ sent envoys with tribute.

sentences.zh: 三月，京師大飢，民相食。

translations.literal: Third month, the capital region had great famine; the people ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month famine gripped the capital and people turned to cannibalism.

sentences.zh: 壬辰，公卿詣闕謝。

translations.literal: On the renchen day, dukes and ministers went to the palace gate to apologize.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 April 109 CE the high ministers appeared at the palace gate to apologize.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『朕以幼沖，奉承鴻業，不能宣流風化，而感逆陰陽，至令百姓飢荒，更相噉食。

translations.literal: An edict said: 'We, being young and tender, receive and uphold the vast enterprise, unable to spread and flow custom and transformation, yet have moved yin and yang contrary—so as to cause the hundred surnames famine and hunger, mutually to devour one another.
translations.idiomatic: An edict ran: I am young and bear the great charge, yet I have failed to harmonize yin and yang, until the people starve and feed on each other.

sentences.zh: 永懷悼歎，若墜淵水。

translations.literal: Forever cherishing mourning and sighing, as if falling into deep water.
translations.idiomatic: I mourn endlessly, as one drowning in a pit.

sentences.zh: 咎在朕躬，非群司之責，而過自貶引，重朝廷之不德。

translations.literal: The fault is in Our person, not the host of offices' responsibility—yet excessively they demote and withdraw themselves, doubling the court's lack of virtue.
translations.idiomatic: The fault is mine, not the ministers', yet their self-blame only deepens the court's shame.

sentences.zh: 其務思變復，以助不逮。

translations.literal: Let them strive to think of change and restoration, thereby to assist Our insufficiency.
translations.idiomatic: Let them think how to set things right and help where I fall short.

sentences.zh: 』癸巳，詔以鴻池假與貧民。

translations.literal: ' On the guisi day, it was ordered that Hong Pool be lent to poor commoners.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 April the Hong Pool preserve was opened to the poor for gathering food.

sentences.zh: 壬寅，司徒魯恭免。

translations.literal: On the renyin day, Minister of Education Lu Gong was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 April 109 CE Minister of Education Lu Gong was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙寅，大鴻臚九江夏勤為司徒。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, bingyin day, Grand Herald Xia Qin of Jiujiang became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 May 109 CE Xia Qin of Jiujiang, Grand Herald, became Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 三公以國用不足，奏令吏人入錢穀，得為關內侯、虎賁羽林郎、五大夫、官府吏、緹騎、營士各有差。

translations.literal: The Three Dukes, because state expenditure was insufficient, memorialized ordering clerks and commoners to contribute cash and grain, thereby to become marquis within the passes, tiger-runner Forest of Feathers gentlemen, fifth-grade grandee, government-office clerks, cavalry escorts, and camp soldiers, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Dukes, short of revenue, allowed purchase of rank and posts—marquis within the passes, guardsmen, fifth-grade titles, clerical posts, and camp duty—at graded prices in cash and grain.

sentences.zh: 己巳，詔上林、廣成苑可墾闢者，賦與貧民。

translations.literal: On the jisi day, it was ordered that Shanglin and Guangcheng parks where land could be opened for tillage be allotted to poor commoners.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 May arable land in Shanglin and Guangcheng parks was granted to the poor.

sentences.zh: 甲申，清河王虎威薨。

translations.literal: On the jiashen day, King Huwei of Qinghe died.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 June 109 CE Prince Huwei of Qinghe died.

sentences.zh: 五月丙申，封樂安王寵子延平為清河王。

translations.literal: Fifth month, bingshen day, the son Yanping of King Chong of Le'an was enfeoffed as king of Qinghe.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 June 109 CE Yanping, son of Prince Chong of Le'an, was made prince of Qinghe.

sentences.zh: 丁酉，沛王正薨。

translations.literal: On the dingyou day, King Zheng of Pei died.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 June King Zheng of Pei died.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，京師大風。

translations.literal: On the guichou day, the capital region had great wind.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 July a violent wind struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 六月，烏桓寇代郡、上谷、涿郡。

translations.literal: Sixth month, the Wuhuan raided Dai, Shanggu, and Zhuo commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the Wuhuan raided Dai, Shanggu, and Zhuo.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，海賊張伯路等寇略緣海九郡，遣侍御史龐雄督州、郡兵討破之。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, sea bandits Zhang Bolu and others raided and plundered nine commanderies along the coast; Attending Imperial Clerk Pang Xiong was sent to supervise provincial and commandery troops to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn Zhang Bolu's pirates raided nine coastal commanderies; Pang Xiong led provincial forces and defeated them.

sentences.zh: 庚子，詔長吏案行在所，皆令種宿麥蔬食，務盡地力，其貧者給種餉。

translations.literal: On the gengzi day, it was ordered that senior clerks inspect the places where they are, all ordering to plant winter wheat and vegetable food, striving to exhaust land power; to the poor grant seed and rations.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 August magistrates were ordered to tour their districts, plant winter wheat and vegetables to use every acre, and supply seed to the poor.

sentences.zh: 九月，鴈門烏桓及鮮卑叛，敗五原郡兵於高渠谷。

translations.literal: Ninth month, Yanmen Wuhuan and the Xianbi rebelled, defeating Wuyuan commandery troops at Gaoqu Valley.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Yanmen Wuhuan and Xianbi rebels defeated Wuyuan troops at Gaoqu Valley.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，南單于叛，圍中郎將耿种於美稷。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, the Southern Chanyu rebelled, surrounding General of the Gentlemen of the Household Geng Zhong at Meiji.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter the Southern Chanyu rebelled and besieged Geng Zhong at Meiji.

sentences.zh: 十一月，遣行車騎將軍何熙討之。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, Acting General of Chariots and Cavalry He Xi was sent to punish him.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month He Xi was dispatched as acting general of chariots and cavalry against him.

sentences.zh: 十二月辛酉，郡國九地震。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, xinyou day, nine commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On 30 December 109 CE nine commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 乙亥，有星孛於天苑。

translations.literal: On the yihai day, there was a broom star in the Celestial Park.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 January 110 CE a comet appeared in the Celestial Park constellation.

sentences.zh: 是歲，京師及郡國四十一雨水雹。

translations.literal: That year, the capital region and forty-one commanderies and kingdoms had rain, water, and hail.
translations.idiomatic: That year the capital and forty-one commanderies suffered rainstorms and hail.

sentences.zh: 幷、涼二州大饑，人相食。

translations.literal: The two provinces of Bing and Liang had great famine; people ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: Bing and Liang provinces starved until people ate one another.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月元日，會，徹樂，不陳充庭車。

translations.literal: Fourth year, spring, first month, new year's day, assembly; music was removed; the filling-the-court carriages were not displayed.
translations.idiomatic: On New Year's Day, 110 CE, the court met without music and without the usual display of tribute carriages.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，詔以三輔比遭寇亂，人庶流冗，除三年逋租、過更、口筭、芻稾；

translations.literal: On the xinmao day, it was ordered that because the Three Adjuncts have recently suffered bandit turmoil, the people drift and scatter—remove three years' overdue rent, substitute levy, mouth poll, and fodder straw;
translations.idiomatic: On 16 February an edict remitted three years of back taxes, substitute service levy, poll tax, and fodder dues in the Three Adjuncts, where people had fled banditry;

sentences.zh: 稟上郡貧民各有差。

translations.literal: grain was granted to poor commoners of Shang commandery, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: and grain was distributed on a graded scale to the poor in Shang commandery.

sentences.zh: 海賊張伯路復與勃海、平原劇賊劉文河、周文光等攻厭次，殺縣令，遣御史中丞王宗督青州刺史法雄討破之。

translations.literal: Sea bandit Zhang Bolu again with Bohai and Pingyuan fierce bandits Liu Wenhe, Zhou Wenguang, and others attacked Yanci, killed the county magistrate; Palace Secretary Wang Zong was sent to supervise Governor of Qingzhou Fa Xiong to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bolu joined Liu Wenhe and Zhou Wenguang of Bohai and Pingyuan in attacking Yanci and killing the magistrate; Wang Zong and Fa Xiong, governor of Qingzhou, defeated them.

sentences.zh: 度遼將軍梁慬、遼東太守耿夔討破南單于於屬國故城。

translations.literal: General Who Crosses the Liao Liang Jin and Governor of Liaodong Geng Kui attacked and defeated the Southern Chanyu at the old city of the dependent state.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin and Geng Kui defeated the Southern Chanyu at the old dependent-state city.

sentences.zh: 丙午，詔減百官及州、郡、縣奉各有差。

translations.literal: On the bingwu day, it was ordered to reduce the hundred officials' and provinces', commanderies', and counties' salaries, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 March 110 CE salaries of central and local officials were cut on a graded scale.

sentences.zh: 二月丁巳，稟九江貧民。

translations.literal: Second month, dingsi day, grain was granted to poor commoners of Jiujiang.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 April 110 CE grain was issued to the poor of Jiujiang.

sentences.zh: 南匈奴寇常山。

translations.literal: The Southern Xiongnu raided Changshan.
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Xiongnu raided Changshan commandery.

sentences.zh: 乙丑，初置長安、雍二營都尉官。

translations.literal: On the yichou day, commandant posts for the two camps of Chang'an and Yong were first established.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 April camp commandant offices were created for Chang'an and Yong.

sentences.zh: 乙亥，詔自建初以來，諸祅言它過坐徙邊者，各歸本郡；

translations.literal: On the yihai day, it was ordered that from Jianchu onward, all who by demon talk and other faults sat and were exiled to the border—each return to his home commandery;
translations.idiomatic: On 23 April an edict ordered everyone exiled to the frontier since Jianchu for heterodox speech or other offenses to return to their home commanderies;

sentences.zh: 其沒入官為奴婢者，免為庶人。

translations.literal: those confiscated into office as slave girls and servants were released to become commoners.
translations.idiomatic: those enslaved in government service were freed as commoners.

sentences.zh: 詔謁者劉珍及《五經》博士，校定東觀《五經》、諸子、傳記、百家蓺術，整齊脫誤，是正文字。

translations.literal: It was ordered that Messenger Liu Zhen and the Five Classics erudits collate and fix at the Eastern View the Five Classics, masters, traditions, records, and the hundred schools' arts and skills, ordering omissions and errors, correcting graphs and characters.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhen and the Five Classics academicians were ordered to collate the Eastern Institute library—classics, philosophers, histories, and technical works—and correct errors in the text.

sentences.zh: 三月，南單于降。

translations.literal: Third month, the Southern Chanyu surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month the Southern Chanyu submitted.

sentences.zh: 先零羌寇褒中，漢中太守鄭勤戰歿。

translations.literal: The Xianling Qiang raided Baozhong; Governor of Hanzhong Zheng Qin died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianling Qiang raided Baozhong; Zheng Qin, governor of Hanzhong, fell in battle.

sentences.zh: 徙金城郡都襄武。

translations.literal: Jincheng commandery was moved, its capital to Xiangwu.
translations.idiomatic: The seat of Jincheng commandery was moved to Xiangwu.

sentences.zh: 戊子，杜陵園火。

translations.literal: On the wuzi day, Du Mausoleum Park caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On 29 May fire broke out at the Du imperial tomb park.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，郡國九地震。

translations.literal: On the guisi day, nine commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 June nine commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，六州蝗。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, six provinces had locusts.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer locusts swarmed six provinces.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On the dingchou day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 17 June a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 秋七月乙酉，三郡大水。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, yiyou day, three commanderies had great flood.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 August 110 CE three commanderies were inundated.

sentences.zh: 己卯，騎都尉任仁下獄死。

translations.literal: On the jimao day, Colonel of Cavalry Ren Ren was cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 September Ren Ren, colonel of cavalry, was jailed and died there.

sentences.zh: 九月甲申，益州郡地震。

translations.literal: Ninth month, jiashen day, Yizhou commandery had an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 October Yizhou commandery was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 冬十月甲戌，新野君陰氏薨，使司空持節護喪事。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, jiaxu day, Lady Xinye nee Yin died; the Minister of Works was sent bearing the staff to supervise funeral affairs.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 October Lady Yin of Xinye died; the Minister of Works supervised her funeral with full honors.

sentences.zh: 大將軍鄧騭罷。

translations.literal: Grand General Deng Zhi was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Grand General Deng Zhi was dismissed from office.

sentences.zh: 五年春正月庚辰朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Fifth year, spring, first month, gengchen new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the first month, 111 CE.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，郡國十地震。

translations.literal: On the bingxu day, ten commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 February ten commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 己丑，太尉張禹免。

translations.literal: On the jichou day, Grand Commandant Zhang Yu was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 February Grand Commandant Zhang Yu was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 甲申，光祿勳李脩為太尉。

translations.literal: On the jiashen day, Household Counselor Li Xiu became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 March Li Xiu, Household Counselor, became Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 二月丁卯，詔省減郡國貢獻太官口食，先零羌寇河東，遂至河內。

translations.literal: Second month, dingmao day, it was ordered to reduce and cut commandery and kingdom tribute and Grand Provisioner mouth rations; the Xianling Qiang raided Hedong, then reached Henei.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 March 111 CE an edict cut tribute to the palace and the Grand Provisioner's commissary; the Xianling Qiang raided Hedong and pushed into Henei.

sentences.zh: 三月，詔隴西徙襄武，安定徙美陽，北地徙池陽，上郡徙衙。

translations.literal: Third month, it was ordered that Longxi move to Xiangwu, Anding to Meiyang, Beidi to Chiyang, Shang to Ya.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month an edict ordered the seats of Longxi, Anding, Beidi, and Shang commanderies moved east to Xiangwu, Meiyang, Chiyang, and Ya.

sentences.zh: 夫餘夷犯塞，殺傷吏人。

translations.literal: Puyŏ Yi violated the passes, killing and wounding clerks and people.
translations.idiomatic: Puyŏ tribesmen raided the frontier, killing and wounding officials and civilians.

sentences.zh: 閏月丁酉，赦涼州河西四郡。

translations.literal: Intercalary month, dingyou day, Liang province's four commanderies west of the River were amnestied.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 May 111 CE the four Hexi commanderies in Liang province received a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 戊戌，詔曰：『朕以不德，奉郊廟，承大業，不能興和降善，為人祈福。

translations.literal: On the wuxu day an edict said: 'We, lacking virtue, serve suburb and temple altars, receive the great enterprise, unable to raise harmony and send down good, to pray blessing for others.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 May 111 CE an edict confessed lack of virtue at the altars and failure to bring harmony or blessings to the people.

sentences.zh: 災異蜂起，寇賊縱橫，夷狄猾夏，戎事不息，百姓匱乏，疲於徵發。

translations.literal: Disasters and prodigies swarm up; bandits and robbers run rampant; Yi and Di trouble the Hua; military affairs do not cease; the hundred surnames are exhausted and worn, weary from levies and calls.
translations.idiomatic: Disasters multiply, bandits roam, barbarians raid the heartland, campaigns never end, and the people are worn out by conscription.

sentences.zh: 重以蝗蟲滋生，害及成麥，秋稼方收，甚可悼也。

translations.literal: Doubled by locusts breeding and multiplying, harming the ripe wheat—autumn crops just being gathered—very lamentable.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts have ruined the standing wheat just as the autumn harvest comes in—deeply grievous.

sentences.zh: 朕以不明，統理失中，亦未獲忠良以毗闕政。

translations.literal: We, being unclear, in ordering and governing miss the center; moreover We have not obtained the loyal and good to assist Our deficient government.
translations.idiomatic: My rule has gone awry, and I have not found loyal ministers to shore up what is lacking.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「顛而不扶，危而不持，則將焉用彼相矣。

translations.literal: The tradition says: "When one stumbles and is not supported, when in peril and not held—then what use are those assistants?
translations.idiomatic: The tradition asks: when the ruler stumbles and you do not steady him, what use are ministers?

sentences.zh: 」公卿大夫將何以匡救，濟斯艱戹，承天誡哉？

translations.literal: " Dukes, ministers, and grandees—with what shall they rescue and save, succor this hardship and peril, receive Heaven's warning?
translations.idiomatic: How will you ministers rescue the throne from this crisis and answer Heaven's warning?

sentences.zh: 蓋為政之本，莫若得人，褒賢顯善，聖制所先。

translations.literal: As for the root of governing, nothing compares to obtaining men; praising worthies and displaying the good is what sage institutions put first.
translations.idiomatic: Good government begins with the right men; exalting the worthy is what sage kings put first.

sentences.zh: 「濟濟多士，文王以寧」。

translations.literal: "Many are the officers in array; by them King Wen had tranquility."
translations.idiomatic: Many officers in array—that was how King Wen kept the realm at peace.

sentences.zh: 思得忠良正直之臣，以輔不逮。

translations.literal: We wish to obtain loyal, good, and upright ministers to assist Our insufficiency.
translations.idiomatic: I seek loyal, upright men to remedy my shortcomings.

sentences.zh: 其令三公、特進、侯、中二千石、二千石、郡守、諸侯相舉賢良方正、有道術、達於政化、能直言極諫之士各一人，及至孝與眾卓異者，并遣詣公車，朕將親覽焉。』

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the Three Dukes, specially advanced, marquises, central two-thousand-dan, two-thousand-dan, commandery governors, and kingdom chancellors each recommend one worthy and good, upright and correct, possessed of the way and arts, penetrating in government transformation, able to speak bluntly and remonstrate to the utmost—and those of utmost filial piety and outstanding from the crowd—together send them to the Public Carriage gate; We will personally peruse them.'
translations.idiomatic: Let the Three Dukes, specially advanced nobles, marquises, two-thousand-dan officials, governors, and princely chancellors each nominate one worthy, learned, politically astute, and outspoken man, and anyone famed for extreme filial piety or outstanding conduct, and send them to the Public Carriage office for my personal review.

sentences.zh: 六月甲辰，樂成王巡薨。

translations.literal: Sixth month, jiachen day, King Xun of Lecheng died.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 July 111 CE Prince Xun of Lecheng died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己巳，詔三公、特進、九卿、校尉，舉列將子孫明曉戰陳任將帥者。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, jisi day, it was ordered that the Three Dukes, specially advanced, Nine Ministers, and colonels raise among ranked generals' grandsons and sons those who clearly understand battle formations and can serve as generals and commanders.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 July the court ordered nominations of generals' descendants skilled in military command.

sentences.zh: 九月，漢陽人杜琦、王信叛，與先零諸種羌攻陷上邽城。

translations.literal: Ninth month, men of Hanyang Du Qi and Wang Xin rebelled, together with the Xianling various-tribe Qiang attacking and taking Shanggui city.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Du Qi and Wang Xin of Hanyang rebelled with the Xianling Qiang and captured Shanggui.

sentences.zh: 十二月，漢陽太守趙博遣客刺殺杜琦。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, Governor of Hanyang Zhao Bo sent retainers who assassinated Du Qi.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Zhao Bo, governor of Hanyang, had retainers assassinate Du Qi.

sentences.zh: 是歲，九州蝗，郡國八雨水。

translations.literal: That year, the nine provinces had locusts; eight commanderies and kingdoms had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: That year locusts struck nine provinces and eight commanderies flooded.

sentences.zh: 六年春正月庚申，詔越巂置長利、高望、始昌三苑，又令益州郡置萬歲苑，犍為置漢平苑。

translations.literal: Sixth year, spring, first month, gengshen day, it was ordered that in Yuexi be established three parks, Changli, Gaowang, and Shichang; moreover Yizhou commandery was ordered to establish Wansui Park; Qianwei to establish Hanping Park.
translations.idiomatic: On 16 February 112 CE an edict created hunting parks in Yuexi, Yizhou, and Qianwei commanderies.

sentences.zh: 三月，十州蝗。

translations.literal: Third month, ten provinces had locusts.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month locusts swarmed ten provinces.

sentences.zh: 夏四月乙丑，司空張敏罷。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, yichou day, Minister of Works Zhang Min was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 May 112 CE Minister of Works Zhang Min was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 己卯，太常劉愷為司空。

translations.literal: On the jimao day, Grand Master of Ceremonies Liu Kai became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 May Liu Kai, Grand Master of Ceremonies, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 五月，旱。

translations.literal: Fifth month, drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month drought returned.

sentences.zh: 丙寅，詔令中二千石下至黃綬，一切復秩還贖，賜爵各有差。

translations.literal: On the bingyin day, it was ordered that from central two-thousand-dan down to yellow-ribbon, all restore rank and return ransom, bestow noble ranks each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 June 112 CE an edict restored ranks and ransoms for officials from central two-thousand-dan down to yellow-ribbon holders and granted graded honorary titles.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，皇太后幸雒陽寺，錄囚徒，理冤獄。

translations.literal: On the wuchen day, the empress dowager favored Luoyang prison with her presence, recorded prisoners, and ordered unjust imprisonments.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 June the empress dowager visited Luoyang jail, reviewed prisoners, and redressed wrongful cases.

sentences.zh: 六月壬辰，豫章、員谿、原山崩。

translations.literal: Sixth month, renchen day, Yuzhang, Yuanxi, and Yuan mountain collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 July mountains collapsed in Yuzhang and at Yuanxi.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On the xinsi day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 August a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 遣侍御史唐喜討漢陽賊王信，破斬之。

translations.literal: Attending Imperial Clerk Tang Xi was sent to punish the Hanyang bandit Wang Xin; he was defeated and beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: Tang Xi defeated the rebel Wang Xin in Hanyang and executed him.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月辛丑，護烏桓校尉吳祉下獄死。

translations.literal: Winter, eleventh month, xinchou day, Colonel Who Protects the Wuhuan Wu Zhi was cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 December Wu Zhi, colonel for the Wuhuan, was jailed and died.

sentences.zh: 是歲，先零羌滇零死，子零昌復襲偽號。

translations.literal: That year, the Xianling Qiang Dianling died; his son Lingchang again usurped the false title.
translations.idiomatic: That year Dianling of the Xianling Qiang died; his son Lingchang again claimed the rebel imperial title.

sentences.zh: 七年春正月庚戌，皇太后率大臣命婦謁宗廟。

translations.literal: Seventh year, spring, first month, gengxu day, the empress dowager led great ministers' titled wives to visit the ancestral temples.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 February 113 CE the empress dowager led the wives of high officials to sacrifice at the ancestral shrines.

sentences.zh: 二月丙午，郡國十八地震。

translations.literal: Second month, bingwu day, eighteen commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 March eighteen commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 夏四月乙未，平原王勝薨。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, yiwei day, King Sheng of Pingyuan died.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 April Prince Sheng of Pingyuan died.

sentences.zh: 丙申晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: Last day bingshen, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse fell on the last day of the fourth month, 25 May 113 CE.

sentences.zh: 五月庚子，京師大雩。

translations.literal: Fifth month, gengzi day, the capital region held a great rain prayer.
translations.idiomatic: On 29 May the capital held a great ceremony for rain.

sentences.zh: 秋，護羌校尉侯霸、騎都尉馬賢破先零羌。

translations.literal: Autumn, Colonel Who Protects the Qiang Hou Ba and Colonel of Cavalry Ma Xian defeated the Xianling Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn Hou Ba and Ma Xian defeated the Xianling Qiang.

sentences.zh: 八月丙寅，京師大風，蝗蟲飛過洛陽。

translations.literal: Eighth month, bingyin day, the capital region had great wind; locusts flew past Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 September 113 CE a gale struck the capital and a swarm of locusts passed over Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 詔賜民爵。

translations.literal: It was ordered to bestow noble ranks on the people.
translations.idiomatic: An edict granted honorary ranks to the people.

sentences.zh: 郡國被蝗傷稼十五以上，勿收今年田租；

translations.literal: In commanderies and kingdoms struck by locusts harming crops fifteen percent or more, do not collect this year's field rent;
translations.idiomatic: Where locusts had destroyed fifteen percent or more of the crop, this year's land tax was waived;

sentences.zh: 不滿者，以實除之。

translations.literal: where it does not reach that, remove according to the actual amount.
translations.idiomatic: lesser damage was remitted in proportion to actual loss.

sentences.zh: 九月，調零陵、桂陽、豫章、會稽租米，賑給南陽、廣陵、下邳、彭城、山陽、廬江、九江饑民；

translations.literal: Ninth month, rent rice of Lingling, Guiyang, Yuzhang, and Kuaiji was transferred to relieve and supply starving people of Nanyang, Guangling, Xiapi, Pengcheng, Shanyang, Lujiang, and Jiujiang;
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month grain tax from Lingling, Guiyang, Yuzhang, and Kuaiji was shipped to feed the hungry in Nanyang, Guangling, Xiapi, Pengcheng, Shanyang, Lujiang, and Jiujiang.

sentences.zh: 又調濱水縣榖輸敖倉。

translations.literal: Moreover grain of Binsui counties was transferred for delivery to Aocang granary.
translations.idiomatic: Grain from counties along the river was also shipped to the Aocang granary.

sentences.zh: 元初元年春正月甲子，改元元初。

translations.literal: First year of Yuanchu, spring, first month, jiazi day, the era name was changed to Yuanchu.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 January 114 CE the era name was changed to Yuanchu.

sentences.zh: 賜民爵，人二級，孝悌、力田人三級，爵過公乘，得移與子若同產、同產子，民脫無名數及流民欲占者人一級；

translations.literal: The people were granted noble ranks, each two grades; filial and fraternal models and strength-in-farming, three grades; when rank passed gongcheng, it could be transferred to a son or same-birth sibling or same-birth sibling's son; commoners shedding unregistered numbers and floating people who wished to register, one grade each;
translations.idiomatic: Commoners received two steps of honorary rank, models of filial piety and farming three; rank above gongcheng could pass to a son or sibling; unregistered persons and vagrants who settled received one step;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃貧不能自存者穀，人三斛；

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the seriously ill and crippled poor unable to support themselves, grain, three hu per person;
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely ill, and the destitute received three hu of grain each;

sentences.zh: 貞婦帛，人一匹。

translations.literal: to chaste wives, silk, one bolt per person.
translations.idiomatic: women honored for chastity received one bolt of silk each.

sentences.zh: 二月己卯，日南地坼。

translations.literal: Second month, jimao day, Rinan ground split.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 March 114 CE the earth split open in Rinan commandery.

sentences.zh: 三月癸酉，日有食之。

translations.literal: Third month, guiyou day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse occurred on 12 April 114 CE.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丁酉，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, dingyou day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 May 114 CE a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 京師及郡國五旱、蝗。

translations.literal: The capital region and five commanderies and kingdoms had drought and locusts.
translations.idiomatic: The capital and five commanderies suffered drought and locusts.

sentences.zh: 詔三公、特進、列侯、中二千石、二千石、郡守舉敦厚質直者，各一人。

translations.literal: It was ordered that the Three Dukes, specially advanced, ranked marquises, central two-thousand-dan, two-thousand-dan, and commandery governors each raise one who is honest, thick, plain, and upright.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Dukes, nobles, and governors were each ordered to nominate one man noted for honesty and plainspoken integrity.

sentences.zh: 五月，先零羌寇雍城。

translations.literal: Fifth month, the Xianling Qiang raided Yong city.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month the Xianling Qiang raided Yong.

sentences.zh: 六月丁巳，河東地陷。

translations.literal: Sixth month, dingsi day, Hedong ground sank.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 June 114 CE the earth subsided in Hedong.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，蜀郡夷寇蠶陵，殺縣令。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, Shu commandery Yi raided Canling and killed the county magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Yi tribesmen of Shu attacked Canling and killed the magistrate.

sentences.zh: 九月乙丑，太尉李脩罷。

translations.literal: Ninth month, yichou day, Grand Commandant Li Xiu was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 September 114 CE Grand Commandant Li Xiu was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 先零羌寇武都、漢中，絕隴道。

translations.literal: The Xianling Qiang raided Wudu and Hanzhong, cutting the Long road.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianling Qiang raided Wudu and Hanzhong and severed the Long route.

sentences.zh: 辛未，大司農山陽司馬苞為太尉。

translations.literal: On the xinwei day, Grand Minister of Agriculture Sima Bao of Shanyang became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 September Sima Bao of Shanyang, Grand Minister of Agriculture, became Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 冬十月戊子朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, wuzi new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the tenth month, 12 October 114 CE.

sentences.zh: 先零羌敗涼州刺史皮陽於狄道。

translations.literal: The Xianling Qiang defeated Governor of Liangzhou Pi Yang at Didao.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianling Qiang defeated Pi Yang, governor of Liangzhou, at Didao.

sentences.zh: 乙卯，詔除三輔三歲田租、更賦、口筭。

translations.literal: On the yimao day, it was ordered to remove three years' field rent, substitute levy, and mouth poll from the Three Adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 November an edict remitted three years of land tax, substitute levy, and poll tax in the Three Adjuncts.

sentences.zh: 十一月。

translations.literal: Eleventh month.
translations.idiomatic: The eleventh month passed without further entry in the annals.

sentences.zh: 是歲，郡國十五地震。

translations.literal: That year, fifteen commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: That year fifteen commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，詔稟三輔及幷、涼六郡流冗貧人。

translations.literal: Second year, spring, first month, it was ordered to grant grain to floating redundant poor people of the Three Adjuncts and the six commanderies of Bing and Liang.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 115 CE, the court issued grain to vagrants and the poor in the Three Adjuncts and six Bing and Liang commanderies.

sentences.zh: 蜀郡青衣道夷奉獻內屬。

translations.literal: The Qingyi-road Yi of Shu commandery presented tribute and attached inward.
translations.idiomatic: The Yi of the Qingyi road in Shu sent tribute and submitted.

sentences.zh: 修理西門豹所分漳水為支渠，以溉民田。

translations.literal: They repaired the branch canals into which Ximen Bao had divided the Zhang River, to irrigate the people's fields.
translations.idiomatic: The branch canals that Ximen Bao had cut from the Zhang River were repaired to water the fields.

sentences.zh: 二月戊戌，遣中謁者收葬京師客死無家屬及棺椁朽敗者，皆為設祭；

translations.literal: Second month, wuxu day, inner messengers were sent to gather and bury in the capital those who died as guests without kin and those whose coffins were rotten; for all sacrifices were set.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 March 115 CE the court sent agents to bury strangers who had died in the capital without family and those with decayed coffins, and offered sacrifices for them;

sentences.zh: 其有家屬，尤貧無以葬者，賜錢人五千。

translations.literal: those who had kin but were especially poor with no means to bury—cash was bestowed, five thousand per person.
translations.idiomatic: families too poor to bury their dead received five thousand cash each.

sentences.zh: 辛酉，詔三輔、河內、河東、上黨、趙國、太原各修理舊渠，通利水道，以溉公私田疇。

translations.literal: On the xinyou day, it was ordered that the Three Adjuncts, Henei, Hedong, Shangdang, Zhao state, and Taiyuan each repair old canals, clear and benefit watercourses, to irrigate public and private fields.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 April an edict ordered the Three Adjuncts, Henei, Hedong, Shangdang, Zhao, and Taiyuan to dredge old canals and irrigate public and private fields.

sentences.zh: 三月癸亥，京師大風。

translations.literal: Third month, guihai day, the capital region had great wind.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 May 115 CE a violent wind struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 先零羌寇益州，遣中郎將尹就討之。

translations.literal: The Xianling Qiang raided Yizhou; General of the Gentlemen of the Household Yin Jiu was sent to punish them.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianling Qiang raided Yi province; Yin Jiu was sent against them.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙午，立貴人閻氏為皇后。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, bingwu day, Honored Lady nee Yan was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 June Lady Yan the Honored Lady was made empress.

sentences.zh: 五月，京師旱，河南及郡國十九蝗。

translations.literal: Fifth month, the capital region had drought; Henan and nineteen commanderies and kingdoms had locusts.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month the capital and Henan suffered drought while nineteen commanderies were hit by locusts.

sentences.zh: 甲戌，詔曰：『朝廷不明，庶事失中，灾異不息，憂心悼懼。

translations.literal: On the jiaxu day an edict said: 'The court is not enlightened; the multitude of affairs misses the center; disasters and prodigies do not cease; Our heart mourns and fears.
translations.idiomatic: On 20 July 115 CE an edict admitted that the government had erred and omens would not stop; the ruler grieved and feared.

sentences.zh: 被蝗以來，七年於茲，而州、郡隱匿，裁言頃畝。

translations.literal: Since being struck by locusts, seven years have passed here, yet provinces and commanderies conceal and hide, only speaking of qing-mu of land.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts have plagued us for seven years, yet provinces underreport damage to a few acres.

sentences.zh: 今群飛蔽天，為害廣遠，所言所見，寧相副邪？

translations.literal: Now swarms fly covering the sky; the harm is broad and far—what is said and what is seen—could they match?
translations.idiomatic: Now the sky is black with locusts and the damage is vast—how can your reports match what we see?

sentences.zh: 三司之職，內外是監，即不奏聞，又無舉正。

translations.literal: The Three Offices' duty is to supervise inner and outer; they neither memorialize nor have impeachment and correction.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Offices should oversee all, yet they neither report nor impeach wrongdoing.

sentences.zh: 天灾至重，欺罔辠大。

translations.literal: Heaven's disaster is utmost heavy; deceiving and falsehood's crime is great.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's calamity is grave, and lying about it is a serious crime.

sentences.zh: 今方盛夏，且復假貸，以觀厥後。

translations.literal: Now it is just high summer; for the time being again grant lent grace, thereby to observe what follows.
translations.idiomatic: In this high summer I grant temporary leniency and will watch what follows.

sentences.zh: 其務消救灾眚，安輯黎元。』

translations.literal: Let them strive to dispel and extinguish disaster and ill, pacify and gather the black-haired masses.'
translations.idiomatic: Work to end this calamity and bring peace to the people.

sentences.zh: 六月丙戌，太尉司馬苞薨。

translations.literal: Sixth month, bingxu day, Grand Commandant Sima Bao died.
translations.idiomatic: On 29 July 115 CE Grand Commandant Sima Bao died.

sentences.zh: 洛陽新城地裂。

translations.literal: Luoyang new city ground split.
translations.idiomatic: The earth gaped open in the new city district of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 秋七月辛巳，太僕太山馬英為太尉。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, xinsi day, Grand Coachman Ma Ying of Taishan became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 August Ma Ying of Taishan, Grand Coachman, became Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 八月，遼東鮮卑圍無慮縣。

translations.literal: Eighth month, Liaodong Xianbi surrounded Wulü county.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Xianbi of Liaodong besieged Wulü county.

sentences.zh: 九月，又攻夫犁營，殺縣令。

translations.literal: Ninth month, they again attacked the Fuli camp and killed the county magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month they attacked the Fuli garrison and killed the magistrate.

sentences.zh: 壬午晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: Last day renwu, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse fell on the last day of the ninth month, 21 October 115 CE.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，遣中郎將任尚屯三輔。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, General of the Gentlemen of the Household Ren Shang was sent to encamp in the Three Adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter Ren Shang was sent to garrison the Three Adjuncts.

sentences.zh: 詔郡國中都官繫囚減死一等，勿笞，詣馮翊、扶風屯，妻子自隨，占著所在；

translations.literal: It was ordered that in commanderies and kingdoms capital-office imprisoned convicts have death reduced one grade, not be flogged, go to encamp in Zuopingyi and Youfufeng, wives and children following of themselves, registering where they are;
translations.idiomatic: An edict commuted capital convicts in central prisons by one degree, spared the rod, and sent them to farm garrisons in Zuopingyi and Youfufeng with their families;

sentences.zh: 女子勿輸。

translations.literal: women are not to be transported.
translations.idiomatic: women were exempt from transport labor.

sentences.zh: 亡命死辠以下贖，各有差。

translations.literal: Fugitives with warrant and death crime downward—ransom, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: Fugitives and lesser capital cases could commute sentences with graded ransoms.

sentences.zh: 其吏人聚為盜賊，有悔過者，除其罪。

translations.literal: Among clerks and commoners who gathered as bandits and robbers, those who repent their fault—remove their crime.
translations.idiomatic: Commoners who had joined bandits but repented were pardoned.

sentences.zh: 乙未，右扶風仲光、安定太守杜恢、京兆虎牙都尉耿溥與先零羌戰於丁奚城，光等大敗，並沒。

translations.literal: On the yiwei day, Youfufeng Zhong Guang, Governor of Anding Du Hui, and Jingzhao Tiger-Fang Colonel Geng Pu fought the Xianling Qiang at Dingxi city; Guang and the others were greatly defeated and all perished.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 November Zhong Guang of Youfufeng, Du Hui of Anding, and Geng Pu were defeated by the Xianling Qiang at Dingxi and all died in battle.

sentences.zh: 左馮翊司馬鈞下獄，自殺。

translations.literal: Zuopingyi Sima Jun was cast into prison and committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Jun of Zuopingyi was jailed and killed himself.

sentences.zh: 十一月庚申，郡國十地震。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, gengshen day, ten commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 December 115 CE ten commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 十二月，武陵澧中蠻叛，州、郡擊破之。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, the Man of Wuling Lizhong rebelled; province and commandery troops attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the Man of Lizhong in Wuling rebelled; provincial forces defeated them.

sentences.zh: 己酉，司徒夏勤罷。

translations.literal: On the jiyou day, Minister of Education Xia Qin was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 December Minister of Education Xia Qin was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 庚戌，司空劉愷為司徒，光祿勳袁敞為司空。

translations.literal: On the gengxu day, Minister of Works Liu Kai became Minister of Education; Household Counselor Yuan Chang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 December Liu Kai moved from Minister of Works to Minister of Education, and Yuan Chang from Household Counselor to Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月甲戌，修理太原舊溝渠，溉灌官私田。

translations.literal: Third year, spring, first month, jiaxu day, old ditches and canals of Taiyuan were repaired, irrigating public and private fields.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 February 116 CE Taiyuan's old irrigation works were repaired to water public and private fields.

sentences.zh: 東平陸上言木連理。

translations.literal: Lu of Dongping submitted upward word that trees had joined trunks.
translations.idiomatic: Lu in Dongping reported the omen of intertwined tree trunks.

sentences.zh: 蒼、鬱林、合浦蠻夷反叛，二月，遣侍御史任逴督州、郡兵討之。

translations.literal: Cang, Yulin, and Hepu Man and Yi rebelled; second month, Attending Imperial Clerk Ren Tong was sent to supervise provincial and commandery troops to punish them.
translations.idiomatic: The Man and Yi of Cangwu, Yulin, and Hepu rebelled; in the second month Ren Tong led provincial forces against them.

sentences.zh: 郡國十地震。

translations.literal: Ten commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: Ten commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 三月辛亥，日有食之。

translations.literal: Third month, xinhai day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse occurred on 26 March 116 CE.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，赦蒼梧、鬱林、合浦、南海吏人為賊所迫者。

translations.literal: On the bingchen day, clerks and people of Cangwu, Yulin, Hepu, and Nanhai who were forced by bandits were amnestied.
translations.idiomatic: On 31 March officials and commoners in the southern commanderies who had been coerced by rebels were pardoned.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，京師旱。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, the capital region had drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer the capital suffered drought.

sentences.zh: 五月，武陵蠻復叛，州、郡討破之。

translations.literal: Fifth month, Wuling Man again rebelled; province and commandery troops attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month the Wuling Man rebelled again and were defeated by provincial troops.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，度遼將軍鄧遵率南匈奴擊先零羌於靈州，破之。

translations.literal: On the guiyou day, General Who Crosses the Liao Deng Zun led the Southern Xiongnu to attack the Xianling Qiang at Lingzhou and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 June Deng Zun led Southern Xiongnu allies to defeat the Xianling Qiang at Lingzhou.

sentences.zh: 越巂徼外夷舉種內屬。

translations.literal: Yi beyond the passes of Yuexi raised their tribes and attached inward.
translations.idiomatic: Tribes beyond Yuexi submitted wholesale to Han.

sentences.zh: 六月，中郎將任尚遣兵擊破先零羌於丁奚城。

translations.literal: Sixth month, General of the Gentlemen of the Household Ren Shang sent troops to attack and defeat the Xianling Qiang at Dingxi city.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Ren Shang's troops defeated the Xianling Qiang at Dingxi.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，武陵蠻復叛，州、郡討平之。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, Wuling Man again rebelled; province and commandery troops attacked and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn the Wuling Man rebelled again and were pacified.

sentences.zh: 緱氏地坼。

translations.literal: Goushi ground split.
translations.idiomatic: The earth split open at Goushi.

sentences.zh: 九月辛亥，趙王宏薨。

translations.literal: Ninth month, xinhai day, King Hong of Zhao died.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Prince Hong of Zhao died.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，蒼梧、鬱林、合浦蠻夷降。

translations.literal: Winter, eleventh month, Cangwu, Yulin, and Hepu Man and Yi surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month of winter the Man and Yi of Cangwu, Yulin, and Hepu submitted.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，初聽大臣、二千石、刺史行三年喪。

translations.literal: On the bingxu day, it was first permitted that great ministers, two-thousand-dan, and regional inspectors observe three years' mourning.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 December high ministers, two-thousand-dan officials, and regional inspectors were for the first time allowed to observe the full three-year mourning for parents.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，郡國九地震。

translations.literal: On the guimao day, nine commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 December nine commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 十二月丁巳，任尚遣兵擊破先零羌於北地。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, dingsi day, Ren Shang sent troops to attack and defeat the Xianling Qiang in Beidi.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 January 117 CE Ren Shang's troops defeated the Xianling Qiang in Beidi.

sentences.zh: 四年春二月乙巳朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Fourth year, spring, second month, yisi new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the second month, 18 February 117 CE.

sentences.zh: 乙卯，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On the yimao day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 February a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 壬戌，武庫灾。

translations.literal: On the renxu day, the armory had a disaster.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 March fire struck the imperial armory.

sentences.zh: 夏四月戊申，司空袁敞薨。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, wushen day, Minister of Works Yuan Chang died.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 April 117 CE Minister of Works Yuan Chang died.

sentences.zh: 己巳，鮮卑寇遼西，遼西郡兵與烏桓擊破之。

translations.literal: On the jisi day, the Xianbi raided Liaoxi; Liaoxi commandery troops together with Wuhuan attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 May Xianbi raided Liaoxi; local troops and Wuhuan allies drove them off.

sentences.zh: 五月丁丑，太常李郃為司空。

translations.literal: Fifth month, dingchou day, Grand Master of Ceremonies Li He became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 May Li He, Grand Master of Ceremonies, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 六月戊辰，三郡雨雹。

translations.literal: Sixth month, wuchen day, three commanderies rained hail.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 June hailstorms struck three commanderies.

sentences.zh: 京師及郡國十雨水。

translations.literal: The capital region and ten commanderies and kingdoms had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: The capital and ten commanderies were inundated by rain.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『今年秋稼茂好，垂可收獲，而連雨未霽，懼必淹傷。

translations.literal: An edict said: 'This year's autumn crops are lush and good, about to be gathered, yet continuous rain has not cleared—We fear they must be flooded and harmed.
