translations.idiomatic: An edict noted that the autumn crop looked fine and nearly ready to harvest, yet endless rain threatened to ruin it in the fields.

sentences.zh: 夕惕惟憂，思念厥咎。

translations.literal: Evening fearful only anxious, pondering its fault.
translations.idiomatic: I lie awake anxious, searching for the fault.

sentences.zh: 夫霖雨者，人怨之所致。

translations.literal: As for soaking rain—it is what people's resentment brings about.
translations.idiomatic: Prolonged rain, they say, follows from popular resentment.

sentences.zh: 其武吏以威暴下，文吏妄行苛刻，鄉吏因公生姦，為百姓所患苦者，有司顯明其罰。

translations.literal: Those military clerks who by might tyrannize below, civil clerks who rashly practice harsh severity, village clerks who on public business breed wickedness, who are what the hundred surnames suffer and bitterly—let the responsible offices clearly manifest their punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Let harsh military clerks, cruel civil officials, and corrupt petty clerks who torment the people be punished openly.

sentences.zh: 又《月令》「仲秋養衰老，授几杖，行糜粥」。

translations.literal: Moreover the 'Monthly Ordinances' says, "In mid-autumn nurture the aged and infirm, grant stools and staffs, distribute gruel."
translations.idiomatic: The Monthly Ordinances require that in mid-autumn the aged receive staffs and gruel.

sentences.zh: 方今案比之時，郡、縣多不奉行。

translations.literal: Just now at the time of household review, commanderies and counties mostly do not carry it out.
translations.idiomatic: At the annual household review, many counties ignore the rules.

sentences.zh: 雖有糜粥，糠秕相半，長吏怠事，莫有躬親，甚違詔書養老之意。

translations.literal: Though there is gruel, chaff and husks are half of it; senior clerks are lazy in affairs, none personally attending—this greatly contravenes the edict's intent to nurture the old.
translations.idiomatic: The gruel is half chaff, magistrates neglect their duty, and none of this matches the edict's intent to care for the elderly.

sentences.zh: 其務崇仁恕，賑護寡獨，稱朕意焉。』

translations.literal: Let them strive to exalt benevolence and forbearance, relieve and protect widowers and solitaries—accord with Our intent.'
translations.idiomatic: Practice mercy, relieve the widowed and alone, and so fulfill my wishes.

sentences.zh: 九月，護羌校尉任尚使客刺殺叛羌零昌。

translations.literal: Ninth month, Colonel Who Protects the Qiang Ren Shang had retainers assassinate the rebel Qiang Lingchang.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Ren Shang had Lingchang, the rebel Qiang leader, assassinated by agents.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月己卯，彭城王恭薨。

translations.literal: Winter, eleventh month, jimao day, King Gong of Pengcheng died.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 December 117 CE Prince Gong of Pengcheng died.

sentences.zh: 十二月，越巂夷寇遂久，殺縣令。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, Yuexi Yi raided Suijiu and killed the county magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Yi of Yuexi attacked Suijiu and killed the magistrate.

sentences.zh: 甲子，任尚及騎都尉馬賢與先零羌戰於富平上河，大破之。

translations.literal: On the jiazi day, Ren Shang and Colonel of Cavalry Ma Xian fought the Xianling Qiang at Fuping on the upper river and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 January 118 CE Ren Shang and Ma Xian crushed the Xianling Qiang at Fuping on the upper river.

sentences.zh: 虔人羌率眾降，隴右平。

translations.literal: The Qianren Qiang led the multitude in surrender; the Long right was pacified.
translations.idiomatic: The Qianren Qiang submitted in force, and the Longxi region was pacified.

sentences.zh: 是歲，郡國十三地震。

translations.literal: That year, thirteen commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: That year thirteen commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 五年春正月，越巂夷叛。

translations.literal: Fifth year, spring, first month, Yuexi Yi rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 118 CE, the Yi of Yuexi rebelled.

sentences.zh: 二月壬戌，中山王憲薨。

translations.literal: Second month, renxu day, King Xian of Zhongshan died.
translations.idiomatic: On 17 March 118 CE Prince Xian of Zhongshan died.

sentences.zh: 三月，京師及郡國五旱，詔稟遭旱貧人。

translations.literal: Third month, the capital region and five commanderies and kingdoms had drought; it was ordered to grant grain to poor people struck by drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month the capital and five commanderies suffered drought; an edict issued grain to the poor.

sentences.zh: 夏六月，高句驪與穢貊寇玄菟。

translations.literal: Summer, sixth month, Gaogouli together with Huimo raided Xuantu.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Koguryŏ and Huimo raided Xuantu commandery.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，越巂蠻夷及旄牛豪叛，殺長吏。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, Yuexi Man and Yi and the Maoniu chieftains rebelled, killing senior clerks.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn the Man and Yi of Yuexi and the Maoniu chieftains rebelled and killed local officials.

sentences.zh: 丙子，詔曰：『舊令制度，各有科品，欲令百姓務崇節約。

translations.literal: On the bingzi day an edict said: 'The old ordinances and institutions each have categories and grades, wishing to make the hundred surnames strive to honor thrift and restraint.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 August 118 CE an edict recalled that old laws set graded standards meant to teach the people frugality.

sentences.zh: 遭永初之際，人離荒戹，朝廷躬自菲薄，去絕奢飾，食不兼味，衣無二綵。

translations.literal: Meeting the time of Yongchu, people parted from wasteland and peril; the court personally practiced austerity, removed and cut off lavish ornament, food did not combine flavors, garments had not two colors.
translations.idiomatic: In the Yongchu troubles the court itself lived plainly—no luxury in dress or doubled dishes at meals.

sentences.zh: 比年雖獲豐穰，尚乏儲積，而小人無慮，不圖久長，嫁聚送終，紛華靡麗，至有走卒奴婢被綺縠，著珠璣。

translations.literal: In recent years though harvest has been abundant, still storage is lacking; yet petty men are without care, do not plan long-term; marrying, gathering, sending off the dead—lavish splendor and beauty—so that there are runners and slave girls draped in damask and gauze, wearing pearls and jade.
translations.idiomatic: Though harvests have lately been good, granaries remain thin, yet common folk spend recklessly on weddings and funerals—even runners and maids wear silk and pearls.

sentences.zh: 京師尚若斯，何以示四遠？

translations.literal: If the capital is still thus, how shall We show the four far reaches?
translations.idiomatic: If the capital sets such an example, what lesson does that send to the provinces?

sentences.zh: 設張法禁，懇惻分明，而有司惰任，訖不奉行。

translations.literal: Laws and prohibitions are set out spread, earnest and clear, yet the responsible offices are lazy in duty and to the end do not carry them out.
translations.idiomatic: The laws are clear and strict, yet officials shirk them and never enforce them.

sentences.zh: 秋節既立，鷙鳥將用，且復重申，以觀後效。』

translations.literal: The autumn season having been established, fierce birds will soon be used; for the time being again reiterate, thereby to observe later effect.'
translations.idiomatic: Autumn—the season of stern justice—approaches; the order is reiterated, and we shall see results hereafter.

sentences.zh: 八月丙申朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Eighth month, bingshen new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the eighth month, 23 August 118 CE.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇代郡，殺長吏。

translations.literal: The Xianbi raided Dai commandery, killing senior clerks.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi raided Dai and killed local officials.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，鮮卑寇上谷。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, the Xianbi raided Shanggu.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter the Xianbi raided Shanggu.

sentences.zh: 十二月丁巳，中郎將任尚有辠，弃市。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, dingsi day, General of the Gentlemen of the Household Ren Shang had crime; he was exposed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 January 119 CE Ren Shang was executed in the marketplace for his crimes.

sentences.zh: 是歲，郡國十四地震。

translations.literal: That year, fourteen commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: That year fourteen commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 六年春二月乙巳，京師及郡國四十二地震，或坼裂，水泉湧出。

translations.literal: Sixth year, spring, second month, yisi day, the capital region and forty-two commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes; some split open; springs gushed forth.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 February 119 CE the capital and forty-two commanderies shook; fissures opened and springs burst from the ground.

sentences.zh: 壬子，詔三府選掾屬高第，能惠利牧養者各五人，光祿勲與中郎將選孝廉郎寬博有謀、清白行高者五十人，出補令、長、丞、尉。

translations.literal: On the renzi day, it was ordered that the Three Offices select clerks of high rank, able to benefit and shepherd and nurture, five each; the Household Counselor and General of the Gentlemen of the Household select fifty filial-and-incorrupt gentlemen who are broad and learned with strategy, pure and white in conduct and lofty, to go out and supplement as magistrates, chiefs, assistants, and sheriffs.
translations.idiomatic: On 2 March the Three Offices were to pick five able clerks each, and the Household Counselor fifty learned filial-and-incorrupt Gentlemen for county posts as magistrates, chiefs, assistants, and sheriffs.

sentences.zh: 乙卯，詔曰：『夫政，先京師，後諸夏。

translations.literal: On the yimao day an edict said: 'As for government, first the capital, afterward the various Xia.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 March an edict declared that good government begins in the capital and spreads to the realm.

sentences.zh: 《月令》仲春「養幼小，存諸孤」，季春「賜貧窮，賑乏絕，省婦使，表貞女」，所以順陽氣，崇生長也。

translations.literal: The 'Monthly Ordinances' mid-spring "nurture the young and small, preserve the various orphans"; late spring "bestow on the poor and destitute, relieve exhaustion and cutoff, reduce women's corvée, display chaste women"—thereby to comply with yang qi and exalt growth.
translations.idiomatic: The Monthly Ordinances require mid-spring care for children and orphans and late-spring relief for the poor, lighter women's labor, and honor for chaste women—all to harmonize with the growing yang.

sentences.zh: 其賜人尤貧困、孤弱、單獨穀，人三斛；

translations.literal: Let there be bestowed grain on people especially poor and destitute, orphaned and weak, solitary alone, three hu per person;
translations.idiomatic: Grant three hu of grain each to the destitute, orphaned, weak, and alone;

sentences.zh: 貞婦有節義十斛，甄表門閭，旌顯厥行。』

translations.literal: to chaste wives possessing integrity and righteousness ten hu; distinguish and display their gate-lane, banner-manifest their conduct.'
translations.idiomatic: chaste women of noted integrity receive ten hu and have their gates honored with public recognition.

sentences.zh: 三月庚辰，始立六宗，祀於洛城西北。

translations.literal: Third month, gengchen day, the Six Zong were first established, sacrificed to northwest of Luoyang city.
translations.idiomatic: On 31 March 119 CE the cult of the Six Zong was instituted northwest of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，會稽大疫，遣光祿大夫將太醫循行疾病，賜棺木，除田租、口賦。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, Kuaiji had great plague; a Household Grandee was sent leading Grand Physicians to tour among the sick, bestow coffin wood, remove field rent and mouth levy.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Kuaiji suffered a great plague; imperial physicians toured the sick, gave coffins, and remitted land and poll taxes.

sentences.zh: 沛國，勃海大風，雨雹。

translations.literal: Pei state and Bohai had great wind and rained hail.
translations.idiomatic: Pei and Bohai were hit by gales and hail.

sentences.zh: 五月，京師旱。

translations.literal: Fifth month, the capital region had drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month the capital suffered drought.

sentences.zh: 六月丁丑，樂成王賓薨。

translations.literal: Sixth month, dingchou day, King Bin of Lecheng died.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 July 119 CE Prince Bin of Lecheng died.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，平原王得薨。

translations.literal: On the bingxu day, King De of Pingyuan died.
translations.idiomatic: On 27 July Prince De of Pingyuan died.

sentences.zh: 是歲，永昌、益州蜀郡夷叛，與越巂夷殺長吏，燔城邑，益州刺史張喬討破降之。

translations.literal: That year, Yongchang, Yizhou, and Shu commandery Yi rebelled; together with Yuexi Yi they killed senior clerks, burned walled towns; Governor of Yi Zhang Qiao attacked, defeated, and made them surrender.
translations.idiomatic: That year Yi of Yongchang, Yizhou, and Shu joined Yuexi rebels in killing officials and burning towns; Zhang Qiao, governor of Yi, defeated and forced their submission.

sentences.zh: 永寧元年春正月甲辰，任城王安薨。

translations.literal: First year of Yongning, spring, first month, jiachen day, King An of Rencheng died.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 February 120 CE Prince An of Rencheng died.

sentences.zh: 三月丁酉，濟北王壽薨。

translations.literal: Third month, dingyou day, King Shou of Jibei died.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 April Prince Shou of Jibei died.

sentences.zh: 車師後王叛，殺部司馬。

translations.literal: The Rear Jushi king rebelled, killing the department major.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Rear Jushi rebelled and killed the Han major stationed there.

sentences.zh: 沈氐羌寇張掖。

translations.literal: The Shenshi Qiang raided Zhangye.
translations.idiomatic: The Shenshi Qiang raided Zhangye.

sentences.zh: 六月，沈氐種羌叛，寇張掖，護羌校尉馬賢討沈氐羌，破之。

translations.literal: Sixth month, the Shenshi-tribe Qiang rebelled, raided Zhangye; Colonel Who Protects the Qiang Ma Xian punished the Shenshi Qiang and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the Shenshi Qiang rebelled and raided Zhangye; Ma Xian defeated them.

sentences.zh: 秋七月乙酉朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, yiyou new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the seventh month, 22 July 120 CE.

sentences.zh: 冬十月己巳，司空李郃免。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, jisi day, Minister of Works Li He was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 October Minister of Works Li He was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，衛尉廬江陳褒為司空。

translations.literal: On the guiyou day, Commandant of the Guards Chen Bao of Lujiang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 October Chen Bao of Lujiang, commandant of the guards, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 自三月至是月，京師及郡國三十三大風，雨水。

translations.literal: From the third month to this month, the capital region and thirty-three commanderies and kingdoms had great wind and rain water.
translations.idiomatic: From March through October the capital and thirty-three commanderies suffered storms and flooding.

sentences.zh: 十二月，永昌徼外撣國譴使貢獻。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, beyond the passes of Yongchang the state of Shan sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Shan beyond Yongchang sent tribute envoys.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，司徒劉愷罷。

translations.literal: On the wuchen day, Minister of Education Liu Kai was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 17 December Minister of Education Liu Kai was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 遼西鮮卑降。

translations.literal: Liaoxi Xianbi surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi of Liaoxi submitted.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，太常楊震為司徒。

translations.literal: On the guiyou day, Grand Master of Ceremonies Yang Zhen became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 December Yang Zhen, Grand Master of Ceremonies, became Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 是歲，郡國二十三地震。

translations.literal: That year, twenty-three commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: That year twenty-three commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 夫餘王遣子詣闕貢獻。

translations.literal: The king of Puyŏ sent his son to the palace gate with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Puyŏ sent his son to court with tribute.

sentences.zh: 燒當羌叛。

translations.literal: The Shaodang Qiang rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: The Shaodang Qiang rebelled.

sentences.zh: 建光元年春正月，幽州刺史馮煥率二郡太守討高句驪、穢貊，不克。

translations.literal: First year of Jianguang, spring, first month, Governor of Youzhou Feng Huan led two commandery governors to punish Gaogouli and Huimo; he did not overcome.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 121 CE, Feng Huan, governor of Youzhou, led two commandery governors against Koguryŏ and Huimo without success.

sentences.zh: 二月癸亥，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Second month, guihai day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 March 121 CE a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 賜諸園貴人、王、主、公、卿以下錢、布各有差。

translations.literal: To the various park honored ladies, kings, princesses, dukes, and ministers down, cash and cloth were bestowed, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: Park ladies, princes, princesses, dukes, and ministers received graded gifts of cash and cloth.

sentences.zh: 以公、卿、校尉、尚書子弟一人為郎、舍人。

translations.literal: One son or younger brother of duke, minister, colonel, or Master of Writing was made Gentleman or attendant-in-waiting.
translations.idiomatic: One son from each high minister's, colonel's, or Masters of Writing official's family was appointed Gentleman or palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，穢貊復與鮮卑寇遼東，遼東太守蔡諷追擊，戰歿。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, Huimo again together with Xianbi raided Liaodong; Governor of Liaodong Cai Feng pursued and attacked, died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Huimo joined Xianbi in raiding Liaodong; Cai Feng, the governor, pursued them and fell in battle.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，以廣川幷清河國。

translations.literal: On the bingchen day, Guangchuan was merged into Qinghe state.
translations.idiomatic: On 30 April Guangchuan was merged into Qinghe.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，尊孝德皇元妃耿氏為甘陵大貴人。

translations.literal: On the dingsi day, the Filial and Virtuous Emperor's primary consort nee Geng was honored as senior honored lady of Ganling.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 May Lady Geng, consort of the late Prince Xiao De, was honored as senior lady of Ganling.

sentences.zh: 甲子，樂成王萇有罪，廢為臨湖侯。

translations.literal: On the jiazi day, King Chang of Lecheng had crime; he was deposed to be marquis of Linhu.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 May Prince Chang of Lecheng was stripped of his title and reduced to Marquis of Linhu for his crimes.

sentences.zh: 己巳，令公、卿、特進、侯、中二千石、二千石、郡國守相，舉有道之士各一人。

translations.literal: On the jisi day, it was ordered that dukes, ministers, specially advanced, marquises, central two-thousand-dan, two-thousand-dan, commandery and kingdom governors and chancellors each raise one man possessed of the way.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 May high ministers and governors were each ordered to nominate one man noted for moral learning.

sentences.zh: 賜鰥、寡、孤、獨、貧不能自存者穀，人三斛。

translations.literal: To widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain was bestowed, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: The destitute, widowed, and orphaned received three hu of grain each.

sentences.zh: 甲戌，遼東屬國都尉龐奮，承偽璽書殺玄菟太守姚光。

translations.literal: On the jiaxu day, dependent-state commandant of Liaodong Pang Fen, receiving a forged seal document, killed Governor of Xuantu Yao Guang.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 May Pang Fen, dependent-state commandant of Liaodong, forged an imperial order and murdered Yao Guang, governor of Xuantu.

sentences.zh: 五月庚辰，特進鄧騭及度遼將軍鄧遵，並以譖自殺。

translations.literal: Fifth month, gengchen day, specially advanced Deng Zhi and General Who Crosses the Liao Deng Zun, both by slander committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 May Deng Zhi, specially advanced, and Deng Zun, general who crosses the Liao, killed themselves after slander.

sentences.zh: 丙申，貶平原王翼為都鄉侯。

translations.literal: On the bingshen day, King Yi of Pingyuan was degraded to marquis of Duxiang.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 June Prince Yi of Pingyuan was demoted to Marquis of Duxiang.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己卯，改元建光，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, jimao day, the era name was changed to Jianguang; a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 July the era name became Jianguang and a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 壬寅，太尉馬英薨。

translations.literal: On the renyin day, Grand Commandant Ma Ying died.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 August Grand Commandant Ma Ying died.

sentences.zh: 八月，護羌校尉馬賢討燒當羌於金城，不利。

translations.literal: Eighth month, Colonel Who Protects the Qiang Ma Xian attacked the Shaodang Qiang at Jincheng; it was not favorable.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Colonel Ma Xian, who protected the Qiang, fought the Shaodang tribes at Jincheng and was defeated.

sentences.zh: 甲子，前司徒劉愷為太尉。

translations.literal: On the jiazi day, former Minister of Education Liu Kai became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiazi day former Minister of Education Liu Kai was appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇居庸關，九月，雲中太守成嚴擊之，戰歿。

translations.literal: The Xianbi raided Juyong Pass; in the ninth month Governor of Yunzhong Cheng Yan attacked them and died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi struck Juyong Pass; in the ninth month Cheng Yan, governor of Yunzhong, counterattacked and was killed in action.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑圍烏桓校尉於馬城，度遼將軍耿夔救之。

translations.literal: The Xianbi besieged the Colonel of the Wuhuan at Macheng; General Who Crosses the Liao Geng Kui rescued him.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi surrounded the Wuhuan colonel at Macheng until Geng Kui, the general who crosses the Liao, relieved the siege.

sentences.zh: 戊子，幸衛尉馮石府。

translations.literal: On the wuzi day, He favored the residence of Commandant of the Guards Feng Shi.
translations.idiomatic: On the wuzi day the emperor visited the home of Feng Shi, commandant of the guards.

sentences.zh: 是秋，京師及郡國二十九雨水。

translations.literal: That autumn, the capital region and twenty-nine commanderies and kingdoms had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn the capital and twenty-nine commanderies suffered prolonged rain and flooding.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月己丑，郡國三十五地震，或坼裂。

translations.literal: Winter, eleventh month, jichou day, thirty-five commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes; some split open.
translations.idiomatic: On the jichou day in the eleventh month of winter thirty-five commanderies shook; the earth cracked in places.

sentences.zh: 詔三公以下，各上封事陳得失。

translations.literal: It was ordered that from the Three Dukes downward each submit sealed memorials stating gains and losses.
translations.idiomatic: An edict required every official from the Three Dukes down to file sealed memorials on policy failures and successes.

sentences.zh: 遣光祿大夫案行，賜死者錢，人二千。

translations.literal: Household Counselors were dispatched to inspect; to the dead cash was bestowed, two thousand per person.
translations.idiomatic: Household counselors toured the stricken regions and paid two thousand cash for each person killed.

sentences.zh: 除今年田租。

translations.literal: This year's field rent was remitted.
translations.idiomatic: Land tax for the current year was waived.

sentences.zh: 其被灾甚者，勿收口賦。

translations.literal: Those severely struck by disaster—do not collect mouth levies.
translations.idiomatic: Where disaster was worst, poll taxes were not to be collected.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇玄菟。

translations.literal: The Xianbi raided Xuantu.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi invaded Xuantu commandery.

sentences.zh: 庚子，復斷大臣二千石以上服三年喪。

translations.literal: On the gengzi day, again it was cut off that great ministers of two-thousand-dan and above observe three-year mourning.
translations.idiomatic: On the gengzi day the court again forbade senior officials at two-thousand-dan rank and above from taking the full three-year mourning leave.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，詔三公、特進、侯、卿、校尉，舉武猛堪將帥者各五人。

translations.literal: On the guimao day, it was ordered that the Three Dukes, specially advanced, marquises, ministers, and colonels each raise five men martial and fierce, fit to be generals and commanders.
translations.idiomatic: On the guimao day the Three Dukes, nobles, ministers, and colonels were each told to recommend five bold men suited to military command.

sentences.zh: 丙午，詔京師及郡國被水雨傷稼者，隨頃畝減田租。

translations.literal: On the bingwu day, it was ordered that in the capital region and commanderies and kingdoms where water and rain injured crops, field rent be reduced according to qing and mu.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingwu day an edict cut land tax in proportion to flood-damaged acreage in the capital and affected commanderies.

sentences.zh: 甲子，初置漁陽營兵。

translations.literal: On the jiazi day, Yuyang camp troops were first established.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiazi day a standing garrison was first set up at Yuyang.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月，高句驪、馬韓、穢貊圍玄菟城，夫餘王遣子與州、郡並力討破之。

translations.literal: Winter, twelfth month, Koguryŏ, Mahan, and Huimo besieged the city of Xuantu; the king of Puyŏ sent his son, and together with the province and commandery they attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month of winter Koguryŏ, Mahan, and Huimo besieged Xuantu; the king of Puyŏ sent his heir to join provincial and local forces and break the siege.

sentences.zh: 延光元年春二月，夫餘王遣子將兵救玄菟，擊高句驪、馬韓、穢貊，破之，遂遣使貢獻。

translations.literal: First year of Yanguang, spring, second month, the king of Puyŏ sent his son leading troops to rescue Xuantu; he attacked Koguryŏ, Mahan, and Huimo, defeated them, and then sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of spring in Yanguang 1 (122 CE) the king of Puyŏ sent his son with an army to relieve Xuantu, routed Koguryŏ, Mahan, and Huimo, and afterward sent tribute envoys.

sentences.zh: 三月丙午，改元延光。

translations.literal: Third month, bingwu day, the era name was changed to Yanguang.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingwu day in the third month the reign title was changed to Yanguang.

sentences.zh: 大赦天下。

translations.literal: A great amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty was proclaimed throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 還徙者，復戶邑屬籍。

translations.literal: Those returned from relocation had household, fief, and dependent registers restored.
translations.idiomatic: Exiles who were allowed home had their household registers and noble fiefs restored.

sentences.zh: 賜民爵及三老、孝悌、力田，人二級；

translations.literal: To the people rank was bestowed, and to the three elders, filial-and-fraternal, and exert-field, two grades per person;
translations.idiomatic: Commoners, local elders, and exemplars of filial piety and diligent farming each received two steps of honorary rank;

sentences.zh: 加賜鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人三斛；

translations.literal: In addition to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the gravely infirm, and the poor unable to support themselves, millet was bestowed, three hu per person;
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the seriously disabled, and the destitute were given an extra three hu of grain each;

sentences.zh: 貞婦帛，人二匹。

translations.literal: To chaste wives silk, two bolts per person.
translations.idiomatic: Virtuous widows received two bolts of silk each.

sentences.zh: 夏四月癸未，京師郡國二十一雨雹。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, guiwei day, the capital region and twenty-one commanderies and kingdoms had rain hail.
translations.idiomatic: On the guiwei day in the fourth month of summer hailstorms struck the capital and twenty-one commanderies.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，司空陳褒免。

translations.literal: On the guisi day, Minister of Works Chen Bao was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On the guisi day Chen Bao was removed as minister of works.

sentences.zh: 五月庚戌，宗正彭城劉授為司空。

translations.literal: Fifth month, gengxu day, Director of the Imperial Clan Liu Shou of Pengcheng became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On the gengxu day in the fifth month Liu Shou of Pengcheng, director of the imperial clan, was appointed minister of works.

sentences.zh: 己巳，改樂成國為安平，封河間王開子得為安平王。

translations.literal: On the jisi day, the kingdom of Lecheng was changed to Anping; the son of King Kai of Hejian, De, was enfeoffed as king of Anping.
translations.idiomatic: On the jisi day Lecheng was renamed Anping, and De, son of Prince Kai of Hejian, was made prince of the new domain.

sentences.zh: 六月，郡國蝗。

translations.literal: Sixth month, commanderies and kingdoms had locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed across the commanderies in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月癸卯，京師及郡國十三地震。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, guimao day, the capital region and thirteen commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On the guimao day in the seventh month the capital and thirteen commanderies were shaken by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 高句驪降。

translations.literal: Koguryŏ submitted.
translations.idiomatic: Koguryŏ surrendered.

sentences.zh: 虔人羌叛，攻穀羅城，度遼將軍耿夔討破之。

translations.literal: The Qianren Qiang rebelled, attacked Guluocheng; General Who Crosses the Liao Geng Kui attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Qianren Qiang rose in revolt and attacked Guluocheng until Geng Kui, the general who crosses the Liao, crushed them.

sentences.zh: 八月戊子，陽陵園寢火。

translations.literal: Eighth month, wuzi day, the inner chamber of Yangling park caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On the wuzi day in the eighth month fire broke out in the sleeping hall at Yangling mausoleum park.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，九真言黃龍見無功。

translations.literal: On the xinmao day, Jiuzhen reported a yellow dragon was seen at Wugong.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinmao day Jiuzhen reported a yellow dragon sighting at Wugong.

sentences.zh: 己亥，詔三公、中二千石，舉刺史、二千石、令、長、相，視事一歲以上至十歲，清白愛利、能勑身率下、防姦理煩、有益於人者，無拘官簿。

translations.literal: On the jihai day, it was ordered that the Three Dukes and central two-thousand-dan raise governors, two-thousand-dan, magistrates, chiefs, and chancellors who had held office from one year up to ten years, pure and white, loving benefit, able to restrain themselves and lead subordinates, prevent treachery and manage vexation, beneficial to people—without being bound by official registers.
translations.idiomatic: On the jihai day the Three Dukes and senior central officials were told to recommend governors, county magistrates, and chiefs with one to ten years in post who were incorrupt, public-spirited, and able to lead and keep order, without regard to formal seniority lists.

sentences.zh: 刺史舉所部，郡國太守、相舉墨綬，隱親悉心，勿取浮華。

translations.literal: Governors shall raise from their departments; commandery and kingdom governors and chancellors shall raise black-ribbon; conceal and examine with whole heart; do not take empty splendor.
translations.idiomatic: Governors were to nominate from their jurisdictions, and commandery heads from among black-ribbon officials, after careful secret inquiry—showy candidates were to be rejected.

sentences.zh: 九月甲戌，郡國二十七地震。

translations.literal: Ninth month, jiaxu day, twenty-seven commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiaxu day in the ninth month twenty-seven commanderies were hit by earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，鮮卑寇鴈門、定襄。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, the Xianbi raided Yanmen and Dingxiang.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter the Xianbi raided Yanmen and Dingxiang.

sentences.zh: 十一月，鮮卑寇太原。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, the Xianbi raided Taiyuan.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month the Xianbi struck Taiyuan.

sentences.zh: 燒當羌豪降。

translations.literal: Shaodang Qiang chieftains submitted.
translations.idiomatic: Leaders of the Shaodang Qiang submitted.

sentences.zh: 十二月，九真徼外蠻夷貢獻內屬。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, beyond the Jiuzhen frontier barbarian Yi presented tribute and submitted inwardly.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month tribes beyond Jiuzhen brought tribute and asked to be registered as subjects.

sentences.zh: 是歲，京師及郡國二十七雨水，大風，殺人。

translations.literal: That year, the capital region and twenty-seven commanderies and kingdoms had rain water; great wind killed people.
translations.idiomatic: That year flooding rains soaked the capital and twenty-seven commanderies, and violent winds killed people.

sentences.zh: 詔賜壓、溺死者年七歲以上錢，人二千；

translations.literal: It was ordered to bestow to those crushed or drowned dead, age seven sui and above, cash, two thousand per person;
translations.idiomatic: An edict granted two thousand cash for each victim aged seven sui or older who had been crushed or drowned;

sentences.zh: 其壞敗廬舍、失亡穀食，粟，人三斛；

translations.literal: those whose huts were ruined and broken, who lost grain food, millet, three hu per person;
translations.idiomatic: those who lost homes and stored grain received three hu of millet each;

sentences.zh: 又田被淹傷者，一切勿收田租；

translations.literal: Moreover fields struck by flooding injury—in all cases do not collect field rent;
translations.idiomatic: and wherever fields had been ruined by flood, no land tax was to be collected at all;

sentences.zh: 若一家皆被灾害而弱小存者，郡、縣為收斂之。

translations.literal: If an entire household was struck by disaster and only the weak and young survived, the commandery and county shall gather the remains and bury them.
translations.idiomatic: When disaster killed every adult in a household but left helpless children, the local commandery and county were to gather the dead and arrange burial.

sentences.zh: 虔人羌反，攻穀羅城，度遼將軍耿夔討破之。

translations.literal: The Qianren Qiang rebelled, attacked Guluocheng; General Who Crosses the Liao Geng Kui attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Qianren Qiang rose again, struck Guluocheng, and were crushed by Geng Kui, the general who crosses the Liao.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，旄牛夷叛，寇靈關，殺縣令。

translations.literal: Second year, spring, first month, the Maoniu Yi rebelled, raided Ling Pass, and killed the county magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring in the second year of Yanguang the Maoniu tribes rebelled, stormed Ling Pass, and slew the magistrate.

sentences.zh: 益州刺史蜀郡西部都尉討之。

translations.literal: The Governor of Yizhou and the dependent-state commandant of the western part of Shujun attacked them.
translations.idiomatic: The governor of Yi and the western dependent-state commandant of Shu marched against them.

sentences.zh: 詔選三署郎及吏人能通《古文尚書》、《毛詩》、《穀梁春秋》各一人。

translations.literal: It was ordered to select from gentlemen of the three bureaus and clerks those able to master the Old Text Documents, the Mao Odes, and the Guliang Spring and Autumn, one each.
translations.idiomatic: An edict called for one clerk or gentleman from each of the three bureaus who could expound the Old Text Shangshu, the Mao version of the Odes, and the Guliang commentary on the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，河東、潁川大風。

translations.literal: On the bingchen day, Hedong and Yingchuan had great wind.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingchen day violent winds struck Hedong and Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 夏六月壬午，郡國十一大風。

translations.literal: Summer, sixth month, renwu day, eleven commanderies and kingdoms had great wind.
translations.idiomatic: On the renwu day in the sixth month of summer eleven commanderies were swept by gales.

sentences.zh: 九真言嘉禾生。

translations.literal: Jiuzhen reported excellent grain had grown.
translations.idiomatic: Jiuzhen reported stalks of exceptional grain sprouting in the fields.

sentences.zh: 丙申，北海王普薨。

translations.literal: On the bingshen day, King Pu of Beihai died.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingshen day Prince Pu of Beihai died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，丹陽山崩。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, Danyang mountains collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn landslides struck Danyang.

sentences.zh: 八月庚午，初令三署郎通達經術任牧民者，視事三歲以上，皆得察舉。

translations.literal: Eighth month, gengwu day, it was first ordered that gentlemen of the three bureaus who comprehend classics and arts and are fit to shepherd the people, having held office three years or more, may all be inspected and raised.
translations.idiomatic: On the gengwu day in the eighth month gentlemen of the three bureaus who had served three years or more in posts requiring classical learning and civil rule became eligible for recommendation.

sentences.zh: 九月，郡國五雨水。

translations.literal: Ninth month, five commanderies and kingdoms had rain water.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month five commanderies suffered heavy rain and flooding.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛未，太尉劉愷罷。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, xinwei day, Grand Commandant Liu Kai was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinwei day in the tenth month of winter Grand Commandant Liu Kai was removed from office.

sentences.zh: 甲戌，司徒楊震為太尉，光祿勳東萊劉憙為司徒。

translations.literal: On the jiaxu day, Minister of Education Yang Zhen became Grand Commandant; Household Counselor Liu Xi of Donglai became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiaxu day Yang Zhen was made Grand Commandant and Liu Xi of Donglai, household counselor, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 十一月甲辰，校獵上林苑。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, jiachen day, a battue hunt was held in Shanglin Park.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiachen day in the eleventh month the emperor conducted a great hunt in Shanglin Park.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑敗南匈奴於曼柏。

translations.literal: The Xianbi defeated the Southern Xiongnu at Manbai.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi routed the Southern Xiongnu at Manbai.

sentences.zh: 是歲，分蜀郡西部為屬國都尉。

translations.literal: That year, the western part of Shujun was split off as dependent-state commandant.
translations.idiomatic: That year the western districts of Shu were hived off as a separate dependent-state commandantship.

sentences.zh: 京師及郡國三地震。

translations.literal: The capital region and three commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: Earthquakes shook the capital and three commanderies.

sentences.zh: 三年春二月丙子，東巡狩。

translations.literal: Third year, spring, second month, bingzi day, eastward inspection hunt.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingzi day in the second month of spring in the third year of Yanguang the emperor set out on an eastern tour of inspection.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，告陳留太守，祠南頓君、光武皇帝於濟陽，復濟陽今年田租、芻稟。

translations.literal: On the dingchou day, the Governor of Chenliu was informed to sacrifice to Lord Nandun and Emperor Guangwu at Jiyang; Jiyang's this-year field rent and fodder grain were remitted.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingchou day the governor of Chenliu was told to offer sacrifice at Jiyang to Liu Qin, lord of Nandun, and to Emperor Guangwu, and Jiyang's land tax and fodder levy for the year were waived.

sentences.zh: 庚寅，遣使者祠唐堯於成陽。

translations.literal: On the gengyin day, envoys were dispatched to sacrifice to Tang Yao at Chengyang.
translations.idiomatic: On the gengyin day envoys were sent to honor the sage-king Yao at Chengyang.

sentences.zh: 戊子，濟南上言，鳳皇集臺縣丞霍收舍樹上。

translations.literal: On the wuzi day, Jinan reported that phoenixes had gathered on a tree at the dwelling of Assistant Magistrate Huo Shou of Tai county.
translations.idiomatic: On the wuzi day Jinan reported phoenixes roosting in a tree at the home of Huo Shou, assistant magistrate of Tai county.

sentences.zh: 賜臺長帛五十匹、丞二十匹、尉半之、吏卒人三匹。

translations.literal: To the chief of Tai fifty bolts of silk were bestowed; to the assistant twenty; to the sheriff half thereof; to clerks and soldiers three bolts each.
translations.idiomatic: The magistrate of Tai received fifty bolts of silk, his assistant twenty, the sheriff half that amount, and each clerk or guard three bolts.

sentences.zh: 鳳皇所過亭部，無出今年田租。

translations.literal: In the cantons and precincts through which the phoenix passed, do not issue this year's field rent.
translations.idiomatic: Cantons along the phoenixes' path were excused from paying land tax for the year.

sentences.zh: 賜男子爵，人二級。

translations.literal: To males rank was bestowed, two grades per person.
translations.idiomatic: Every adult male was granted two steps of honorary rank.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，幸太山，柴告岱宗。

translations.literal: On the xinmao day, He favored Mount Tai, burned firewood and announced to the Lord of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinmao day the emperor ascended Mount Tai and performed the burnt-offering rite to announce his deed to the peak.

sentences.zh: 齊王無忌、北海王翼、樂安王延來朝。

translations.literal: King Wuji of Qi, King Yi of Beihai, and King Yan of Le'an came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Princes Wuji of Qi, Yi of Beihai, and Yan of Le'an attended court.

sentences.zh: 壬辰，宗祀五帝於汶上明堂。

translations.literal: On the renchen day, ancestral sacrifice to the Five Thearchs was performed at the Bright Hall above the Wen.
translations.idiomatic: On the renchen day the Five Thearchs received sacrifice at the Bright Hall on the banks of the Wen.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，告祀二祖、六宗，勞賜郡、縣，作樂。

translations.literal: On the guisi day, announcement sacrifice to the two ancestors and six lineages; labor-reward was bestowed on commanderies and counties; music was performed.
translations.idiomatic: On the guisi day sacrifice was announced to the two founding ancestors and the six cults; counties were feasted and rewarded, and music was played.

sentences.zh: 夏四月乙丑，車駕入宮，假於祖禰。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, yichou day, the carriage entered the palace and lodged with the grandfather and father shrines.
translations.idiomatic: On the yichou day in the fourth month of summer the imperial train entered the palace and the emperor lodged before the shrines of his recent forebears.

sentences.zh: 壬戌，沛國言甘露降豐縣。

translations.literal: On the renxu day, Pei kingdom reported sweet dew had fallen at Feng county.
translations.idiomatic: On the renxu day Pei reported sweet dew at Feng county.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，光祿勳馮石為太尉。

translations.literal: On the wuchen day, Household Counselor Feng Shi became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On the wuchen day Feng Shi, household counselor, was appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 五月，南匈奴左曰逐王叛，使匈奴中郎將馬翼討破之。

translations.literal: Fifth month, the Southern Xiongnu left Riyizhu king rebelled; the Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Ma Yi was sent to attack and defeat him.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month the Southern Xiongnu prince of the left Riyizhu rank rebelled; Ma Yi, colonel in charge of the Xiongnu, crushed the revolt.

sentences.zh: 曰南徼外蠻夷內屬。

translations.literal: Beyond the frontier of Rinan, barbarian Yi submitted inwardly.
translations.idiomatic: Tribes beyond Rinan asked to be registered as imperial subjects.

sentences.zh: 六月，鮮卑寇玄菟。

translations.literal: Sixth month, the Xianbi raided Xuantu.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the Xianbi invaded Xuantu.

sentences.zh: 庚午，閬中山崩。

translations.literal: On the gengwu day, Langzhong mountains collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: On the gengwu day a landslide struck Langzhong.

sentences.zh: 辛未，扶風言白鹿見雍。

translations.literal: On the xinwei day, Fufeng reported a white deer was seen at Yong.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinwei day Fufeng reported a white deer sighting at Yong.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，遣侍御史分行青、冀二州灾害，督錄盜賊。

translations.literal: On the xinsi day, attendant censors were dispatched separately to tour the disasters of Qing and Ji two provinces and supervise recording of bandits.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinsi day attendant censors were sent through Qing and Ji to inspect disaster relief and hunt down bandits.

sentences.zh: 秋七月丁酉，初復右校、左校令丞官。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, dingyou day, right and left park commandant assistants' offices were first restored.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingyou day in the seventh month the posts of right- and left-park commandants and their assistants were reinstated.

sentences.zh: 日南徼外蠻豪帥詣闕貢獻。

translations.literal: Beyond the frontier of Rinan, barbarian chieftains came to the palace gate with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Tribal leaders from beyond Rinan brought tribute to the capital.

sentences.zh: 馮翊言甘露降頻陽、衙。

translations.literal: Fengyi reported sweet dew had fallen at Pinyang and Ya.
translations.idiomatic: Fengyi reported sweet dew at Pinyang and Ya.

sentences.zh: 潁川上言木連理。

translations.literal: Yingchuan reported trees with joined trunks.
translations.idiomatic: Yingchuan reported trees whose trunks had grown together—a propitious sign.

sentences.zh: 白鹿、麒麟見陽翟。

translations.literal: White deer and qilin were seen at Yangzhai.
translations.idiomatic: A white deer and qilin were sighted at Yangzhai.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇高柳。

translations.literal: The Xianbi raided Gaoliu.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi struck Gaoliu.

sentences.zh: 梁王堅薨。

translations.literal: King Jian of Liang died.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Jian of Liang died.

sentences.zh: 八月辛巳，大鴻臚耿寶為大將軍。

translations.literal: Eighth month, xinsi day, Grand Herald Geng Bao became Grand General.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinsi day in the eighth month Geng Bao, grand herald, was made Grand General.

sentences.zh: 戊子，潁川上言麒麟一、白虎二見陽翟。

translations.literal: On the wuzi day, Yingchuan reported one qilin and two white tigers were seen at Yangzhai.
translations.idiomatic: On the wuzi day Yingchuan reported one qilin and two white tigers at Yangzhai.

sentences.zh: 九月丁酉，廢皇太子保為濟陰王。

translations.literal: Ninth month, dingyou day, the heir apparent Bao was deposed to be king of Jiyin.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingyou day in the ninth month Crown Prince Liu Bao was demoted to Prince of Jiyin.

sentences.zh: 乙巳，詔郡國中都官死辠繫囚減罪一等，詣敦煌、隴西及度遼營；

translations.literal: On the yisi day, it was ordered that in commanderies and kingdoms capital-office death-crime bound prisoners reduce crime one grade and go to Dunhuang, Longxi, and the Cross-the-Liao camp;
translations.idiomatic: On the yisi day an edict commuted capital-bound convicts in metropolitan jails one degree and banished them to Dunhuang, Longxi, or the Cross-the-Liao garrison;

sentences.zh: 其右趾以下及亡命者贖，各有差。

translations.literal: those from right toe downward and fugitives—ransom, each according to grade.
translations.idiomatic: those guilty of lesser mutilating crimes and fugitives might ransom their sentences by graded payments.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，濟南上言黃龍見歷城。

translations.literal: On the xinhai day, Jinan reported a yellow dragon was seen at Licheng.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinhai day Jinan reported a yellow dragon at Licheng.

sentences.zh: 庚申晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: Gengshen darkening-of-the-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the last day of the month, gengshen.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，行幸長安。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, the progress favored Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter the emperor traveled to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 壬午，新豐上言鳳皇集西界亭。

translations.literal: On the renwu day, Xinfeng reported phoenixes had gathered at the west-border pavilion.
translations.idiomatic: On the renwu day Xinfeng reported phoenixes at a pavilion on the western border.

sentences.zh: 丁亥，會三輔守、令、掾史於長安，作樂。

translations.literal: On the dinghai day, the governors, magistrates, and clerks of the Three Supports were assembled at Chang'an; music was performed.
translations.idiomatic: On the dinghai day the emperor feasted the officials of the metropolitan region at Chang'an to the sound of music.

sentences.zh: 閏月乙未，祠高廟，遂有事十一陵，歷觀上林、昆明池。

translations.literal: Intercalary month, yiwei day, Gao Temple was sacrificed to; then there was service at the eleven mausolea; He toured viewing Shanglin and Kunming Pool.
translations.idiomatic: On the yiwei day of the intercalary month sacrifice was offered at Gaozu's temple, then at the eleven imperial tombs, and the tour continued through Shanglin Park and Kunming Pool.

sentences.zh: 遣使者祠太上皇于萬年，以中牢祠蕭何、曹參、霍光。

translations.literal: Envoys were dispatched to sacrifice to the Supreme Progenitor at Wannian; with middle victims Xiao He, Cao Can, and Huo Guang were sacrificed to.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys sacrificed to Liu Taigong at Wannian and offered medium-grade victims at the shrines of Xiao He, Cao Can, and Huo Guang.

sentences.zh: 十一月乙丑，至自長安。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, yichou day, He arrived from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: On the yichou day in the eleventh month the emperor returned from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 十二月乙未，琅邪言黃龍見諸縣。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, yiwei day, Langye reported yellow dragons were seen in various counties.
translations.idiomatic: On the yiwei day in the twelfth month Langye reported yellow dragons in several counties.

sentences.zh: 是歲，京師及郡國二十三地震；

translations.literal: That year, the capital region and twenty-three commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes;
translations.idiomatic: That year the capital and twenty-three commanderies were shaken by earthquakes;

sentences.zh: 三十六雨水，疾風，雨雹。

translations.literal: thirty-six had rain water, swift wind, and rain hail.
translations.idiomatic: thirty-six saw flooding, violent winds, and hailstorms.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月壬午，東郡言黃龍二、麒麟一見濮陽。

translations.literal: Fourth year, spring, first month, renwu day, Dong commandery reported two yellow dragons and one qilin were seen at Puyang.
translations.idiomatic: On the renwu day in the first month of spring in the fourth year of Yanguang Dong commandery reported two yellow dragons and a qilin at Puyang.

sentences.zh: 二月乙亥，下邳王衍薨。

translations.literal: Second month, yihai day, King Yan of Xiapi died.
translations.idiomatic: On the yihai day in the second month Prince Yan of Xiapi died.

sentences.zh: 甲辰，南巡狩。

translations.literal: On the jiachen day, southward inspection hunt.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiachen day the emperor set out on a southern tour of inspection.

sentences.zh: 三月戊午朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Third month, wuwu new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the third month.

sentences.zh: 庚申，幸宛，帝不豫。

translations.literal: On the gengshen day, He favored Wan; the Emperor was unwell.
translations.idiomatic: On the gengshen day the court reached Wan, and the emperor fell seriously ill.

sentences.zh: 辛酉，令大將軍耿寶行太尉事。

translations.literal: On the xinyou day, it was ordered that Grand General Geng Bao act Grand Commandant affairs.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinyou day Grand General Geng Bao was ordered to discharge the duties of Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 祠章陵園廟，告長沙、零陵太守，祠定王、節侯、鬱林府君。

translations.literal: Zhangling park temples were sacrificed to; the governors of Changsha and Lingling were informed to sacrifice to King Ding, Marquis Jie, and the lord of Yulin prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: Sacrifice was offered at the Zhangling shrines, and the governors of Changsha and Lingling were told to honor King Ding of Changsha, Marquis Jie, and the spirit lord of Yulin.

sentences.zh: 乙丑，自宛還。

translations.literal: On the yichou day, from Wan He returned.
translations.idiomatic: On the yichou day the train withdrew from Wan.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，幸葉，帝崩於乘輿，年三十二。

translations.literal: On the dingmao day, He favored Ye; the Emperor collapsed in the carriage, age thirty-two sui.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingmao day the emperor reached Ye and died in his carriage at the age of thirty-two.

sentences.zh: 秘不敢宣，所在上食問起居如故。

translations.literal: It was kept secret and not dared proclaimed; wherever they were, they presented food and inquired after health as before.
translations.idiomatic: The death was kept secret; attendants continued to serve meals and ask after the emperor's health as if nothing had changed.

sentences.zh: 庚午，還宮。

translations.literal: On the gengwu day, returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On the gengwu day the cortège reentered the palace.

sentences.zh: 辛未夕，乃發喪。

translations.literal: On the xinwei evening, then mourning was proclaimed.
translations.idiomatic: Only that evening of the xinwei day was the emperor's death announced.

sentences.zh: 尊皇后為皇太后。

translations.literal: The empress was honored as empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: The empress was elevated to empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后臨朝，以后兄大鴻臚閻顯為車騎將軍，定策禁中，立章帝孫濟北惠王壽子北鄉侯懿。

translations.literal: The empress dowager faced the court; her elder brother Grand Herald Yan Xian was made General of Chariots and Cavalry; policy was fixed within the forbidden precincts; Yi, Marquis of Beixiang, son of King Hui of Jibei and grandson of Emperor Zhang, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager took the reins of government, appointed her brother Yan Xian, grand herald, as general of chariots and cavalry, decided the succession within the palace, and enthroned Yi, marquis of Beixiang, grandson of Emperor Zhang and son of Prince Hui of Jibei.

sentences.zh: 甲戌，濟南王香薨。

translations.literal: On the jiaxu day, King Xiang of Jinan died.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiaxu day Prince Xiang of Jinan died.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，北鄉侯即皇帝位。

translations.literal: On the yiyou day, Marquis of Beixiang assumed the emperor's position.
translations.idiomatic: On the yiyou day Marquis Yi of Beixiang ascended the throne.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丁酉，太尉馮石為太傅，司徒劉熹為太尉，參錄尚書事；

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, dingyou day, Grand Commandant Feng Shi became Grand Tutor; Minister of Education Liu Xi became Grand Commandant, jointly recording Masters of Writing affairs;
translations.idiomatic: On the dingyou day in the fourth month of summer Feng Shi moved from Grand Commandant to Grand Tutor, Liu Xi from minister of education to Grand Commandant with joint charge of the Masters of Writing;

sentences.zh: 前司空李郃為司徒。

translations.literal: Former Minister of Works Li He became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Former Minister of Works Li He became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，大將軍耿寶、中常侍樊豐、侍中謝惲、周廣、乳母野王君王聖，坐相阿黨，豐、惲、廣下獄死，寶自殺，聖徙鴈門。

translations.literal: On the xinmao day, Grand General Geng Bao, palace attendant regular Feng Feng, palace attendant Xie Yun and Zhou Guang, and wet nurse Lady Wang Sheng of Yewang, by crime of mutual faction—Feng, Yun, and Guang went to prison and died; Bao committed suicide; Sheng was banished to Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinmao day Grand General Geng Bao, the eunuch Feng Feng, attendants Xie Yun and Zhou Guang, and the emperor's wet nurse Wang Sheng were charged with forming a clique; Feng, Xie, and Zhou died in prison, Geng Bao killed himself, and Wang Sheng was exiled to Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 己酉，葬孝安皇帝於恭陵。

translations.literal: On the jiyou day, the Filial and Secure Emperor was buried at Gong Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiyou day Emperor An was laid to rest at the Gong mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 廟曰恭宗。

translations.literal: The temple was called Gongzong.
translations.idiomatic: His temple name was Gongzong.

sentences.zh: 六月乙巳，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Sixth month, yisi day, a great amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On the yisi day in the sixth month a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 詔先帝巡狩所幸，皆半入今年田租。

translations.literal: It was ordered that places the late emperor's inspection hunt had favored—all half this year's field rent.
translations.idiomatic: An edict halved the year's land tax in every district the late emperor had visited on tour.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，西域長史班勇擊車師後王，斬之。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, Western Regions Chief Clerk Ban Yong attacked the rear king of Jushi and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn Ban Yong, chief clerk of the Western Regions, attacked the junior king of Jushi and executed him.

sentences.zh: 丙午，東海王肅薨。

translations.literal: On the bingwu day, King Su of Donghai died.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingwu day Prince Su of Donghai died.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丙午，越巂山崩。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, bingwu day, Yuexi mountains collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingwu day in the tenth month of winter a landslide struck Yuexi.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，少帝薨。

translations.literal: On the xinhai day, the young emperor died.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinhai day the young emperor died.

sentences.zh: 是冬，京師大疫。

translations.literal: That winter, the capital region had great pestilence.
translations.idiomatic: That winter a severe plague swept the capital.

sentences.zh: 論曰：孝安雖稱尊享御，而權歸鄧氏，至乃損徹膳服，克念政道。

translations.literal: Discussion says: Though Emperor Xiao'an was titled honored and received the chariot and reins, yet power returned to the Deng clan; he even reduced and cut food and garments, striving to think on the way of government.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary: Emperor An bore the throne in name, yet real power lay with the Deng; he pared his own table and wardrobe and tried to mind the business of rule.

sentences.zh: 然令自房帷，威不逮遠，始失根統，歸成陵敝。

translations.literal: Yet orders issued from the bed-curtains; awe did not reach far; from the first the root-trunk was lost, ending in mound ruin and exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: Yet edicts came from the women's quarters, authority never reached the frontier, the dynasty lost its footing from the start, and decline ended in the tombs' decay.

sentences.zh: 遂復計金授官，移民逃寇，推咎臺衡，以答天眚。

translations.literal: Then again offices were given by reckoning gold, people were shifted to flee bandits, blame was pushed on the terrace and steelyard, thereby to answer Heaven's calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Then offices were sold for cash, populations were moved to escape raiders, and the chief ministers were blamed to appease Heaven's rebuke.

sentences.zh: 既雲哲婦，亦『惟家之索』矣。

translations.literal: Since it is called a wise wife, also 'it is the cord of the house'—finished.
translations.idiomatic: They called her a wise consort, yet the Classic says such a one is 'the rope that strangles the house'—and so it proved.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：安德不升，秕我王度。

translations.literal: Encomium says: An's virtue did not ascend; it chaffed Our royal norm.
translations.idiomatic: Verse encomium: An's virtue never rose; it spoiled the royal standard.

sentences.zh: 降奪儲嫡，開萌邪蠹。

translations.literal: Degrading and seizing the heir-son, opened sprouting of evil boring-worms.
translations.idiomatic: Deposing the heir opened the door to treachery and rot.

sentences.zh: 馮石承歡，楊公逢怒。

translations.literal: Feng Shi received favor; Lord Yang met rage.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Shi curried favor while Minister Yang earned the court's wrath.

sentences.zh: 彼日而微，遂祲天路。

translations.literal: That sun became dim; then the way of Heaven was overspread with calamity-mist.
translations.idiomatic: The sun grew faint, and an ill omen veiled the throne of Heaven.
